sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: A hint of what's to come for dying malls: Phoenix mall owner sells out as property is rezoned for other uses
feed.summary: Macerich, a mall owner, sold a majority stake in its Paradise Valley Mall in Phoenix for $100 million to real estate developer RED Development. The 92-acre site will be converted into a mixed-use community with homes, offices, and retail, reflecting a broader trend of repurposing struggling malls as online shopping increases. Macerich president Ed Coppola stated the decision made sense for the company as the retail landscape continues to evolve.

feed.title: Kroger maps its new digital ‘ecosystem’
feed.summary: Kroger plans to double its online sales to over $20 billion by the end of 2023, leveraging its Ocado automated customer fulfillment centers and expanding into new markets like Florida. The company aims to improve digital margins through technology, process improvements, and alternative profit streams, despite initial investor skepticism. Kroger is also focusing on enhancing its fresh assortment, private label offerings, and new meal options to drive market share.

feed.title: Rexford doubles 2021 acquisitions with 5 more industrial properties
feed.summary: Rexford Industrial adds 5 more industrial properties to its portfolio, signaling a busy 2021 following a high-acquisition year in 2020. This move continues Rexford's strategy of expanding its industrial real estate holdings.

feed.title: Why Sea Limited Stock Is Still a Buy Right Now
feed.summary: Despite a recent tech sell-off, Sea Limited (NYSE: SE) is presented as a strong buying opportunity due to its rapid revenue growth, particularly in its e-commerce and gaming divisions, and the strong performance of its digital payment business, Sea Money. The article argues that the company was unfairly impacted by the broader tech market correction and is poised for continued growth, expanding into new regions like Latin America.

feed.title: KKR Buys Seattle Building Leased to Amazon for $580 Million
feed.summary: KKR & Co. has acquired a redeveloped former department store in downtown Seattle for $580 million. The building, located at 300 Pine St., has been converted into offices and is leased to Amazon.com Inc.

feed.title: Amazon Sued for Allegedly Failing to Adequately Pay Overtime Wage
feed.summary: Amazon.com Inc., Amazon Logistics Inc., Amazon.com Services Inc., and JARS TD Inc. are being sued by plaintiff Timothy Coleman for alleged violations of the Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA). Coleman claims that Amazon failed to adequately pay him overtime wages despite him regularly working over 40 hours per week as a delivery driver. The lawsuit argues that Amazon exerted significant control over JARS TD Inc. and its drivers, making Amazon a de facto employer responsible for FLSA compliance.

feed.title: Pennsylvania’s first green hydrogen plant planned for Lancaster County
feed.summary: A clean fuel company, Plug Power, plans to construct Pennsylvania's first green hydrogen plant in southern Lancaster County, utilizing power from the Holtwood Dam and water from the Susquehanna River. The facility, expected to be operational by late 2022, will produce 15 metric tons of liquid hydrogen daily for use in heavy-duty trucks and other industrial applications. This project aims to contribute to emissions reduction goals and position Pennsylvania as a leader in hydrogen production, alongside other initiatives nationwide.

feed.title: Virgil’s Handcrafted Dr. Better
feed.summary: Virgil's, a brand of Reed's Inc., has announced the return of its limited-edition Virgil’s Handcrafted Dr. Better soda. This all-natural soda features premium ingredients like tart cherry, sweet raspberry, and spicy cinnamon, and now includes caffeine. It is available exclusively online through the Virgil’s Store and Amazon.

feed.title: Amazon finally acknowledges delivery driver bathroom problem
feed.summary: Amazon has publicly acknowledged that its delivery drivers face challenges accessing restrooms, confirming that reports of drivers urinating in bottles or publicly are likely true. The company's statement addresses a long-standing issue for its delivery workforce.

feed.title: New Fabs Crop Up Globally in 2021 as Chip Crisis Continues
feed.summary: Amidst the ongoing global chip crisis affecting various industries, several major semiconductor manufacturers are announcing significant expansion plans. Intel is investing $20 billion in two new fabs in Arizona and launching Intel Foundry Services, while GlobalFoundries is committing $1.4 billion to boost its global production capacity. SK Hynix has also received approval to build a massive $106.35 billion fab complex in South Korea, primarily for DRAM production.

feed.title: SunOpta to open new Eden Prairie headquarters as it eyes growth
feed.summary: SunOpta is opening a new 65,000-square-foot headquarters in Eden Prairie, which will include expanded office and R&D space. This move signifies a new chapter for the company after a "transformational" year in 2020, during which it sold its organic commodities business for $389 million and focused on plant-based food ingredients. The company is now in a strong financial position and plans significant capital investments and potential acquisitions in alternative dairy.

feed.title: Amazon broke US law by firing employee activists: Labour board
feed.summary: The National Labor Relations Board's prosecutors found that Amazon.com Inc. violated labor law by firing two prominent internal critics, Emily Cunningham and Maren Costa, last year. The pair were terminated after raising concerns about Amazon’s treatment of warehouse workers during the pandemic and for advocating for climate change initiatives and better working conditions. The NLRB plans to file a complaint if Amazon does not settle, asserting that their dismissals were protected under laws for employees who advocate for workplace changes.

feed.title: How This Company Launched an Emergency Online Summer Camp
feed.summary: Synchrony Financial, a consumer finance firm, launched an emergency online summer camp for employees' children in response to childcare challenges during the COVID-19 pandemic. The program, which quickly expanded to include an after-school tutoring component, helped boost employee morale and productivity by offering diverse virtual activities and externship opportunities, demonstrating the company's agile approach to problem-solving.

feed.title: Airbus Ventures Commends Quantum Computing Leader, IonQ, Inc., on Upcoming Public Debut
feed.summary: Airbus Ventures congratulates IonQ, Inc. on its upcoming merger with dMY Technology Group, Inc. III, which will make IonQ the world’s first publicly traded pure-play quantum computing hardware and software company. Airbus Ventures, a key investor, supports IonQ's scalable roadmap towards developing modular quantum computers and addressing complex challenges in various industrial sectors, including aerospace and green energy. IonQ aims for broad quantum advantage by 2025.

feed.title: First Look: The New Sonos Roam Speaker Is Versatile but Pricey | Reviews by Wirecutter
feed.summary: The article provides a first look at the new Sonos Roam speaker, highlighting its versatility as a portable Bluetooth speaker, a smart speaker with voice activation, and an integrated part of a Sonos multiroom system. While impressed by its sound quality and portability, the review notes its high price ($170) and short battery life as potential drawbacks. The Sonos Roam is presented as an ideal entry point for those looking to start a multiroom speaker system.

feed.title: Gap designs fulfillment system to prioritize ‘most valuable’ customers
feed.summary: Gap Inc. is developing a tiered delivery fulfillment system to prioritize its "most valuable lifetime" customers, categorizing delivery speeds into gold, silver, and bronze. This strategy aims to optimize delivery speed based on customer loyalty while managing fulfillment costs effectively. The company's online sales grew significantly last year, reaching over $6 billion, leading to continued investment in digital and distribution center capacity.

feed.title: Gelsinger Leads Emboldened Intel With Ice Lake Launch
feed.summary: Intel's new CEO, Pat Gelsinger, has launched the 3rd Gen Xeon Scalable processors, "Ice Lake," aimed at reasserting the company's dominance in the server chip market. These new chips feature integrated AI acceleration and security capabilities, backed by strong support from OEMs and public cloud providers. Gelsinger also outlined his "IDM 2.0" strategy, including a $20 billion plan to expand manufacturing fabs and the re-establishment of a major annual event, Intel On, to showcase the company's capabilities.

feed.title: Florida Octogenarian Becomes A Billionaire After Investing In Celsius Energy Drink
feed.summary: Carl DeSantis, an 81-year-old Florida entrepreneur, has become a billionaire after his 31% stake in Celsius Holdings surged to $1.2 billion. He initially invested in the sugar-free energy drink about 15 years ago, enduring delisting and years of losses before the stock soared almost 1,000% in 2020. DeSantis, who previously sold his Rexall Sundown company for $1.8 billion, remained committed to Celsius, guiding it through a turnaround with a new CEO and marketing strategy focused on fitness.

feed.title: Targeting Overseas Tax Shelters (Published 2021)
feed.summary: This article discusses how Bristol Myers Squibb used offshore tax shelters in Ireland and other locations to avoid paying an estimated $1.4 billion in U.S. federal income taxes. The Biden administration plans to crack down on these practices by proposing a global minimum corporate tax rate of 21% to fund infrastructure projects. The article explores the history of such tax avoidance schemes, the motivations behind them, and the challenges the Biden administration faces in implementing its new tax policies.

feed.title: Intel unveils highest performing data-center processors, made with Israel team
feed.summary: Intel has launched its new Ice Lake Server processors, which are optimized for cloud and high-end computing and promise 46% higher performance than the previous generation. These processors, developed in Haifa and produced in Kiryat Gat, Israel, include built-in AI capabilities, encryption, and cybersecurity features. All major cloud service providers, including Google, Microsoft, Amazon, and Dell, plan to offer services powered by these new Intel processors in 2021.

feed.title: JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon: Fintech is an 'enormous competitive' threat to banks
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon has identified fintech companies, including Big Tech firms like Amazon and Apple, as a significant competitive threat to traditional banks. He notes that fintechs are rapidly developing user-friendly digital banking products across loans, payments, and investing, leading to banks playing a smaller role in the financial system. Dimon warns of emerging issues like shadow banking and cryptocurrency regulation, advocating for government oversight to create a level playing field for all financial institutions.

feed.title: Celsius Holdings (NASDAQ:CELH): From $5 to $50 Per Share in 12 Months, Can it Go to $500?
feed.summary: This article details an interview with Celsius Holdings (NASDAQ:CELH) CEO John Fieldly and Investor Relations contact Cameron Donahue. They discuss the company's significant growth from $5 to $50 per share in 12 months, attributing success to shifting from an "emotional selling story" to a "financial selling story" based on strong scan data and velocity. The executives highlight distribution expansion, particularly into convenience channels and through a national DSD network, and a broad demographic appeal as key drivers for future success.

feed.title: JPMorgan’s Chief Sees a Boom Coming (Published 2021)
authors: Andrew Ross Sorkin Jason Karaian Sarah Kessler Michael J. de la Merced Lauren Hirsch and Ephrat Livni
feed.summary: JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon predicts a potential economic boom extending into 2023 driven by excess savings, deficit spending, infrastructure plans, and vaccine-induced euphoria, though he warns of potential inflation and aggressive interest rate hikes. He also addresses concerns about the growing influence of shadow banking and big tech in finance, China's perception of American decline, and the complexities of addressing climate change. Dimon's annual letter also touched on remote work reducing office space needs, cybersecurity spending, and specific tax loopholes he believes should be closed.

feed.title: Amazon's Growth at National Landing Has JBG SMITH in a Prime Position
feed.summary: JBG SMITH CEO Matt Kelly discusses the company's strategic alignment with Amazon's growth at National Landing, focusing on the development of a tech-enhanced neighborhood. The article covers the progress of Amazon's HQ2, the importance of placemaking and digital infrastructure like 5G, and JBG SMITH's commitment to affordable housing and diversifying its asset portfolio. Kelly emphasizes the firm's ambition to transform National Landing into a leading live-work destination and address housing equity in the region.

feed.title: The tech billionaire who is putting women first
feed.summary: Whitney Wolfe Herd, founder of dating app Bumble, has become the world's youngest self-made female billionaire, ringing the Nasdaq bell with her baby on her hip. Having previously been a co-founder of Tinder before a sexual harassment case, she created Bumble with the premise that women initiate conversations in heterosexual matches, emphasizing a "kinder, more accountable" online space. Wolfe Herd, who owns an 11.6% stake in Bumble and also heads Badoo, has been notably open about her past experiences and is an advocate for a more respectful online environment, with Bumble actively working against issues like body shaming.

feed.title: 微弱電流で鼻づまりを解消するデバイス、FDA承認｜Beyond Health｜ビヨンドヘルス
feed.summary: 米Tivic Health Systems社が開発したバイオエレクトロニクス健康デバイス「ClearUP Sinus Pain Relief」が、アレルギーやインフルエンザ、風邪による鼻づまりに対してもFDAの承認を取得しました。このデバイスは微弱電流を使用し、薬を使わずに副鼻腔の痛みや鼻づまりを緩和する携帯型機器で、臨床研究では鼻づまりの改善効果が示されています。現在、米国および海外で特許申請が進められており、既にCEマークも取得済みで、190カ国以上での販売が期待されています。

feed.title: AB InBev, Werner Enterprises to test Embark driverless truck technology
feed.summary: Start-up Embark is partnering with major companies like AB InBev and Werner Enterprises to test and integrate its autonomous trucking technology within the ground freight industry. Embark, which has raised $117 million, aims to license its driverless systems to cargo haulers, focusing on making jobs safer and meeting e-commerce demands. The system, compatible with major truck manufacturers, targets long-haul "middle miles" to improve driver quality of life by allowing them more local routes.

feed.title: Genworth Financial terminates merger deal with Chinese company
feed.summary: Genworth Financial has terminated its $2.7 billion merger agreement with China Oceanwide Holdings Group, a deal that had been in the works for over four years. The decision was made due to numerous delays, primarily caused by state and federal insurance regulators, and will allow Genworth to pursue its revised strategic plan, including a potential partial initial public offering of its U.S. business. The company, which employs thousands in Virginia, expressed disappointment but emphasized the need for clarity regarding its future ownership.

feed.title: Target to spend more than $2B in Black-owned businesses by 2025
feed.summary: Target has announced a commitment to invest over $2 billion in Black-owned businesses by 2025, which includes supporting various enterprises from marketing agencies to construction companies. The initiative also features the "Forward Founders" program, offering expertise and workshops to help Black entrepreneurs develop products and expand their retail presence. This move is part of Target's broader efforts toward social justice and racial equity, including increasing Black team member representation and supporting programs to dismantle systemic racism.

feed.title: Best Buy launches new $200 membership program with free device installation to fight Amazon
feed.summary: Best Buy is launching a new $199.99 annual membership program called "Best Buy Beta" which includes free device installations, tech support, free shipping, extended returns, and exclusive sales. This initiative aims to retain customers in the competitive electronics market and generate recurring revenue, similar to Amazon Prime and Walmart+. The program will pilot in about 60 stores across six states and is expected to eventually replace Best Buy's existing Total Tech Support program.

feed.title: Amazon Adding More Than 700 CNG Trucks
feed.summary: Amazon is expanding its fleet with over 700 compressed natural gas (CNG) trucks to reduce emissions. These new Class 6 and Class 8 trucks will utilize engines supplied by Cummins Westport, a joint venture focused on natural gas engine technology. This move aligns with the trucking sector's increasing shift towards cleaner fuels to meet climate goals, especially given the spike in delivery activity due to COVID-19.

feed.title: Corsair K70 RGB TKL Review: Little Wonder
feed.summary: The Corsair K70 RGB TKL is lauded as a powerful, compact gaming keyboard, praised for its durable build, premium keycaps, and useful media controls. While its 8,000 Hz polling rate's benefit wasn't immediately apparent to the reviewer, the keyboard's overall quality and features, including per-key RGB customization and a tournament switch, justify its $140 price tag for competitive gamers. Its TKL form factor and responsive Cherry MX switches contribute to an enhanced gaming experience by reducing hand fatigue and providing ample mouse space.

feed.title: El Paso businessman Paul Foster drops on Forbes' latest billionaires list
feed.summary: El Paso businessman Paul Foster dropped to No. 1,833 on Forbes' latest list of the world's billionaires, despite his net worth increasing from $1.4 billion in 2020 to $1.7 billion in 2021. He remains the only El Pasoan on the list, which is topped by Jeff Bezos and Elon Musk. Foster's wealth primarily comes from the sale of Western Refining and his subsequent ventures in real estate development, construction, oil and gas, and sports team ownership.

feed.title: Ubiquiti Promised Premium, Secure Routers; Then They Were Hacked
feed.summary: Ubiquiti, a company specializing in networking hardware, experienced a security breach that compromised customer data. The incident highlights the risks associated with cloud-based hardware that forces users to create online accounts, a trend also seen with products from Amazon and Google. Security experts suggest that locally controlled hardware is a more secure alternative, as it offers users more control over their data and privacy.

feed.title: UNFI launches its digital marketplace
feed.summary: United Natural Foods, Inc. (UNFI) has launched its new digital marketplace, Community Marketplace, to expand its product assortment beyond core items. This platform, engineered by Mirakl and accessible via UNFI’s Easy Options site, focuses on offering "hyperlocal" products from emerging brands to small and medium-sized retailers. The initiative aims to meet growing online demand and align with the "endless aisles" strategies adopted by other major grocers like Kroger and Albertsons.

feed.title: Three Philadelphians make Forbes' 2021 billionaire's list: Victoria Mars, Brian Roberts and Richard Hayne
feed.summary: Forbes has released its 2021 World's Billionaires List, revealing that despite global economic turmoil caused by the pandemic, the world's wealthiest individuals have become 86% richer. Three Philadelphians, Victoria Mars, Brian Roberts, and Richard Hayne, made the list, representing major companies like Mars Inc., Comcast, and Urban Outfitters. The list grew significantly this year, adding 660 new billionaires, including a record number of women.

feed.title: Betting on Small Companies Yielded Big Returns (Published 2021)
feed.summary: This article highlights how three small-cap value funds achieved significant returns in the first quarter of 2021 by taking on higher risks. Fund managers from Kinetics Small-Cap Opportunities, Hartford Small-Cap Value, and American Century Small-Cap Value discuss their investment strategies, which include concentrated holdings, focus on free cash flow, and identifying undervalued companies in various sectors. The funds' performance significantly outpaced the S&P 500, demonstrating the potential rewards of small-cap value investing.

feed.title: Amazon will open delivery center in former Haldex facility in Blue Springs
feed.summary: Amazon will open its ninth Kansas City-area facility, a 70,000-square-foot delivery center, in late 2021. The new center will be located in the former Haldex Brake Products Corp. building in Blue Springs. This expansion marks another significant investment by Amazon in the Kansas City metropolitan area.

feed.title: QVC complex in West Hempfield sold for $15M; future tenant could create up to 1,000 jobs
feed.summary: A New York-based real estate investment firm, Saadia Group, has purchased the QVC distribution center in West Hempfield for $15.5 million. Saadia Group is negotiating a lease with an unidentified tenant expected to create 300 to 1,000 jobs, largely replacing the 1,100 jobs lost due to QVC's wind-down. The tenant is slated to begin operations by the end of the year in the 765,000-square-foot complex.

feed.title: Flash deal: 50% off H&R Block tax software for Mac, Windows
feed.summary: B&H Photo is offering 50% off H&R Block 2020 Deluxe + State tax software for Mac and Windows for 24 hours. The deal reduces the price from $44.95 to $22.49, significantly cheaper than Amazon or H&R Block's own pricing. This flash sale comes as the federal tax deadline was extended to May 17, providing an opportunity for those still needing to complete their taxes.

feed.title: Garmin co-founder Min Kao lands on Forbes’ top billionaires list once again
authors: Ellen Cagle - Kansas City Business Journal
feed.summary: Garmin Ltd. co-founder and Executive Chairman Min Kao was once again included in Forbes' list of top billionaires for 2021, with a net worth of $4.5 billion. Despite his net worth increasing by $1 billion, his ranking on the list fell from 538 last year to 638. Forbes calculates net worths using stock prices and exchange rates from March 5 for its annual list.

feed.title: Nvidia announces Omniverse Enterprise suite for real-time, photorealistic graphics design
feed.summary: Nvidia introduced Omniverse Enterprise, a new suite designed for real-time collaborative 3D design and graphics rendering. This platform enables geographically dispersed teams to work together on complex projects, leveraging photorealistic rendering and real-time ray tracing. Key components include Omniverse Nucleus, Connectors for third-party apps, and Nvidia's own Create and View applications, with companies like BMW Group and Ericsson already using it for digital twin creation and 5G network simulation.

feed.title: Nvidia to directly challenge Intel with Arm-based 'Grace' server chip
feed.summary: Nvidia Corp announced its plan to develop an Arm-based server processor chip called "Grace," directly challenging Intel in the data center market. This move intensifies competition with Intel and adds complexity to the antitrust review of Nvidia's $40 billion acquisition of Arm. The new server chip, expected in 2023, is designed for artificial intelligence algorithms, further solidifying Nvidia's focus on AI computing.

feed.title: Domino's Enlists Robots to Help Deliver Pizza in Houston
feed.summary: Domino's is launching autonomous pizza delivery in Houston, partnering with Nuro, a self-driving delivery company. Select customers in the Woodland Heights area can have their pizza delivered by the Nuro R2 robot, the first autonomous, passengerless on-road delivery vehicle approved by the Department of Transportation. This initiative aims to help Domino's learn how customers interact with the robots and how autonomous delivery impacts store operations.

feed.title: MMIT Payer Portrait: UnitedHealthcare
feed.summary: UnitedHealthcare (UHC) is the largest health insurer in the U.S., significantly expanding its presence across various markets, including Medicare Advantage. Its pharmacy benefits are managed by OptumRx, which has driven industry-wide integration through acquisitions like Diplomat Specialty Pharmacy and DivvyDose. UHC's planned acquisition of Change Healthcare faces regulatory scrutiny due to antitrust concerns raised by the American Hospital Association.

feed.title: Impact Shares NAACP Minority Empowerment ETF (NACP) Stock Price, Quote, News & History
feed.summary: This article provides detailed financial information, news, and historical data for the Impact Shares NAACP Minority Empowerment ETF (NACP). It includes key statistics like AUM, dividend, volume, and P/E ratio, along with recent news and a FAQ section covering how to buy, competitors, and dividend information. The ETF focuses on investing in large and mid-capitalization companies that empower minorities, aiming for similar risk and return characteristics to the Morningstar US Large-Mid Cap Index.

feed.title: Amazon confirms plans for three Charlotte-area locations, including one fulfillment center over 1 million square feet
feed.summary: Amazon has announced plans for three new facilities in the Charlotte area: a fulfillment center in Pineville and two delivery stations in Charlotte. These projects are expected to create hundreds of jobs and will collectively span over 1.3 million square feet. The Pineville fulfillment center will be part of the new 288-acre Carolina Logistics Park.

feed.title: Guide to Budget Rental Car's Fastbreak Program
feed.summary: Budget Rent a Car's Fastbreak program is a free loyalty program that offers benefits like faster reservations, expedited car returns, and access to unique promotions and discounts. While it doesn't offer free rentals directly, members can earn rewards coupons (Budget Bucks) and accumulate points or miles with airline and hotel loyalty programs. The article also provides other tips for maximizing discounts on Budget rentals, such as using specific credit cards, linking an Amazon account, and comparing prices through third-party services like Costco Travel.

feed.title: Aldi, Costco Among World's Most Reputable Companies
feed.summary: Aldi and Costco have been recognized among the top 100 most reputable companies globally by RepTrak, making their first appearance on the list. The annual ranking, based on a survey of over 68,000 respondents, highlights the increasing importance of ESG initiatives and overall high reputation scores, especially in a challenging year. RepTrak's findings also show differences in perception across generations, with Millennials being the most positive and Gen Z the most skeptical.

feed.title: Sarasota's Earth's Ally brings organic products to home gardeners
feed.summary: Earth's Ally, a Sarasota-based company founded in 2018, offers organic gardening products for home gardeners, initially sourcing solutions used by large organic farms. The company, led by CEO Scott Allshouse, has developed its own nontoxic weed killer and other plant care products, emphasizing safety for pollinators, pets, and children. Earth's Ally recently secured a nationwide distribution deal with Lowe's and aims to combat the misconception that natural products are ineffective.

feed.title: HP OfficeJet Pro 9015e All-in-One Printer Review
feed.summary: The HP OfficeJet Pro 9015e All-in-One Printer is an Editors' Choice award recipient, lauded for its excellent output quality, fast printing speeds, and competitive running costs, especially when paired with HP's Instant Ink program. Designed for light to medium-duty use in small offices, its main drawback is a single paper input source. The printer offers a feature-rich experience, including an auto-duplexing ADF and diverse connectivity options, making it a strong value proposition for budget-conscious small offices despite its minor flaws.

feed.title: The Biggest E-Commerce Players From China
feed.summary: This article identifies the biggest e-commerce players in China, highlighting Alibaba's dominant position through Taobao and Tmall in terms of global merchandise volumes. JD.com is noted as Alibaba's closest domestic competitor, known for its drone delivery efforts. While Alibaba's newer global platform, AliExpress, exists, its sales do not match those of its regional Chinese platforms.

feed.title: In Depth: Can China Overtake U.S. to Become World’s Top Consumer?
feed.summary: China is rapidly closing the gap with the U.S. to become the world's largest consumer market, driven by its large population and economic growth projections. While it leads in some categories like cars and smartphones, China still lags in overall household consumption, particularly in services and per capita spending. Achieving top consumer status depends on increasing household income, addressing income inequality, reducing high precautionary savings, and making housing more affordable.

feed.title: Daytona area's Hyatt Brown, Jim France make Forbes billionaires list
feed.summary: Forbes' 2021 billionaires list includes two prominent figures from Daytona Beach: J. Hyatt Brown, chairman of Brown & Brown Inc., with an estimated net worth of $2 billion, and Jim France, chairman and CEO of NASCAR, valued at $1.8 billion. The article details their business achievements, philanthropic efforts, and how their wealth has evolved over time, noting their contributions to the local community. It also touches on Jeff Bezos retaining the top spot globally and Florida's overall representation on the list.

feed.title: Manager Question of the Month: How Active Fund Managers Cope With Index Concentration
feed.summary: This article discusses the challenges active fund managers face due to increasing index concentration, particularly in U.S. large-cap growth benchmarks dominated by a few technology companies. It explores how managers adapt their strategies, including considering a switch from diversified to nondiversified funds, and the trade-offs involved in maintaining performance against highly concentrated indexes while adhering to SEC diversification rules. The piece highlights various approaches taken by investment firms like T. Rowe Price and Wellington Management.

feed.title: The Fastest-Growing Sport Might Surprise You
feed.summary: Esports are rapidly growing, attracting a massive global viewership and disrupting traditional sports. Despite its significant growth and investment from companies like Amazon (who bought Twitch for $970 million in 2014), the esports market is still early in its development, valued at $1.1 billion in 2021. Investors can gain exposure to this industry through ETFs like the Global X Video Games & Esports ETF (Nasdaq: HERO).

feed.title: Swvl’s co-founder and former CTO Ahmed Sabbah launches Telda, a Venmo-like app for Gen Z in Egypt
feed.summary: Ahmed Sabbah, co-founder and former CTO of Swvl, has launched Telda, a fintech app aimed at Gen Z in Egypt, similar to Venmo. Telda offers digital accounts with a free Mastercard-powered card for online and in-store payments, cash withdrawals, and facilitates easy money transfers between users. The app has partnered with a local bank to operate in Egypt and plans to expand to Saudi Arabia soon.

feed.title: Data processing units accelerate infrastructure performance
feed.summary: Data processing units (DPUs) are emerging as a critical technology in data centers, offloading networking and communication tasks from CPUs to enhance infrastructure performance. This article explains how DPUs work, highlights key vendors like Fungible, Nvidia, AWS, and Nebulon, and discusses their role in software-defined infrastructure and future enterprise adoption.

feed.title: For Two Vanguard ETFs, the Growth vs. Value Battle Roars On
feed.summary: The article highlights the ongoing "growth vs. value" debate by comparing two Vanguard ETFs: VUG (Growth) and VTV (Value). While VTV has shown stronger short-term performance with a significant year-to-date gain, VUG has historically outperformed VTV over longer periods, driven by its heavy tech holdings. The piece discusses the investment approaches and expense ratios of both funds.

feed.title: Getlabs, an at-home medical labs company, launches with a $3 million raise
feed.summary: Getlabs, a company offering at-home blood-drawing services, has secured $3 million in seed funding led by PivotNorth Capital. The company aims to eliminate the inconvenience of traditional lab visits, as 70% of medical decisions rely on lab results, but 30% of patients skip them due to inconvenience. The funding will support expansion into Phoenix, Philadelphia, and Dallas, with plans to partner with telemedicine services for a fully at-home healthcare experience.

feed.title: Blue Nile Adds Chief Operating Officer, Other Execs
feed.summary: Blue Nile has announced several new executive appointments, including Jason Moss as Chief Operating Officer, Ben Abitbol as Senior Vice President of Supply Chain, and Anita Natarajan as Senior Vice President of International. The company also appointed Dominique Bourgault as Chief Financial Officer and Camille Cleveland as Senior Vice President and General Counsel in March 2020. These hires reflect Blue Nile's focus on strengthening its leadership team and expanding its global reach, particularly in China.

feed.title: LGI Homes Opens Single-Family Home Sales at Pony Lake near Tacoma
feed.summary: LGI Homes has launched sales for 39 new single-family homes at Pony Lake in Puyallup, Washington, near Tacoma. These homes, starting in the low $500,000s, offer various floor plans from 1,500 to 2,400 square feet with modern upgrades and convenient access to major employers and freeways. The community also features on-site amenities and views of Mount Rainier.

feed.title: Dell announces long-awaited spinoff of VMware in $9B deal
feed.summary: Dell Technologies Inc. announced the long-expected spinoff of VMware Inc., its majority-owned computing virtualization company, in a deal set to create two standalone companies. The move, aimed at simplifying capital structures and unlocking shareholder value, will see VMware distribute a cash dividend of $11.5 billion to $12 billion, with $9.3 billion to $9.7 billion going to Dell to help pay down debt. While the two companies will remain commercially linked, analysts believe the separation will boost stock prices and allow both entities to pursue independent growth opportunities.

feed.title: Kroger Delivery Introduces America's First Customer Fulfillment Center
feed.summary: Kroger is launching America's first Customer Fulfillment Center (CFC) in Monroe, OH, powered by Ocado Group's technology, to transform grocery e-commerce. This initiative leverages вертикал integration, machine learning, and robotics to offer affordable, fast delivery of fresh food, building on Kroger's digital business growth. The partnership with Ocado aims to expand Kroger's reach and provide seamless fulfillment capabilities across the U.S.

feed.title: SK Telecom to split into mobile and non-mobile units
feed.summary: SK Telecom will split into two entities: a surviving unit focused on mobile, AI, and digital infrastructure, and a new investment firm overseeing non-telecom businesses like semiconductor, e-commerce, and mobility. This spin-off aims to boost the company's valuation, simplify its structure for increased investment in growth areas, and comply with upcoming revisions to the Fair Trade Act. The new entity will also be responsible for the IPOs of several affiliates, including SK Hynix, 11Street, ADT Caps, and T Map Mobility.

feed.title: Exclusive: Amazon leases part of a new Santa Clara office building
feed.summary: Amazon has leased a portion of a new six-story office building in Santa Clara for its consumer division and AWS cloud unit. The building is part of the Thirty75 Tech project, which offers close to 260,000 square feet of Class A office space. This move follows Amazon's prior adjustments in its Bay Area real estate, including AWS retaining employees in East Palo Alto despite some space shedding.

feed.title: Inside Kroger's big bet on growing home delivery this spring
feed.summary: Kroger announced a major expansion of its grocery home delivery service with the opening of its first automated customer fulfillment center in Monroe, Ohio, part of a plan to build 20 such centers nationwide. These facilities, powered by robots, aim to provide a more reliable, consistent, and potentially less expensive service for customers, enhancing Kroger's digital shopping presence which more than doubled in 2020. The company anticipates these centers will increase job opportunities and further integrate home delivery into customer shopping habits alongside traditional in-store visits.

feed.title: Amazon plans delivery station in Airway Heights
feed.summary: Amazon.com Inc. plans to open a new delivery station in Airway Heights, following the cancellation of a previous project in North Spokane. The company anticipates creating hundreds of full- and part-time jobs, paying at least $15 an hour, along with additional driving opportunities. This new facility will be located in a 187,000-square-foot warehouse and represents a $15 million tenant improvement investment.

feed.title: 2020 Letter to Shareholders
feed.summary: Jeff Bezos reflects on Amazon's growth since 1997, detailing the immense value created for shareholders, employees, third-party sellers, and customers in 2020, totaling $301 billion. He outlines new commitments for Amazon to become "Earth’s Best Employer" and "Earth’s Safest Place to Work," and discusses the company's aggressive climate change initiatives, including The Climate Pledge. Bezos concludes his final annual shareholder letter as CEO by emphasizing the importance of maintaining distinctiveness and embracing the continuous effort required for innovation and progress.

feed.title: Emails arrive from state with $79 payments from Equifax settlement
feed.summary: The Indiana Attorney General's office is alerting consumers that emails offering $79 payments from the Equifax settlement are legitimate for those who opted for electronic restitution. These payments are being distributed in batches to over 236,000 current and former Indiana residents who filed eligible claims. Consumers who did not sign up for the settlement but receive such emails should exercise caution before clicking links.

feed.title: Kroger’s new Ocado centers are powerful. But can they deliver?
feed.summary: Kroger has officially launched its Ocado-powered automated customer fulfillment centers, highlighting their advanced robotics and AI for order fulfillment. While these centers promise efficiency and cost savings for online grocery, questions remain about their ability to meet growing same-day delivery demands and compete in markets where Kroger has a limited physical presence. The company is making a significant investment in this digital ecosystem to transform its operations and capture a larger share of the rapidly expanding online grocery market.

feed.title: New F1 Insights Powered by AWS Will Help FORMULA 1 Fans Make Sense of Split-Second Decisions on the Track
feed.summary: Amazon Web Services (AWS) and FORMULA 1 (F1) are introducing six new F1 Insights powered by AWS for the 2021 racing season, bringing the total to 18 AWS-powered stats. These real-time racing statistics, displayed as on-screen graphics, utilize machine learning and over 1.1 million data points per second from each car to enhance the fan experience by providing deeper understanding of driver and team strategies and performance. The first new stat, Braking Performance, will debut at the GRAND PRIX in Italy, offering insights into drivers' braking styles and their impact on cornering.

feed.title: Never run out of printer ink again with HP’s Instant Ink
feed.summary: HP has launched its Instant Ink subscription service in Australia, aiming to prevent users from running out of printer ink unexpectedly. The service automatically ships ink to customers when supplies are low, offering flexible monthly plans based on page usage and savings of up to 50% on ink costs. To promote the launch, HP has partnered with Manu Feildel for a social media contest encouraging Australians to share family recipes.

feed.title: 100k Club: Digital news subscriptions top 23m, Press Gazette research finds
feed.summary: Press Gazette's latest 100k Club ranking reveals that English-language publishers now collectively boast over 23 million digital news subscriptions. This growth is driven by 28 outlets with 100,000 or more digital-only subscribers, led by The New York Times, Washington Post, and Wall Street Journal. The research also highlights that these publishers could generate over $6 billion annually in digital subscription revenues if all subscribers paid full-rate costs.

feed.title: Rackspace Technology Defines a New Category in Cloud Services
feed.summary: Rackspace Technology (NASDAQ: RXT) has launched Rackspace Elastic Engineering, a new service model designed to help customers build and operate modern cloud environments. This new approach shifts from traditional fixed-scope managed services to providing flexible, modern, cloud-native engineering services through dedicated "Pods" of architects and engineers. Rackspace Elastic Engineering aims to support customers in their cloud-native transformations and DevOps-focused operating models across AWS, Microsoft Azure, and Google Cloud Platform.

feed.title: Wipro clocks its best quarterly results in a decade in Q4 FY21
feed.summary: Wipro announced its best fourth-quarter results in nearly a decade, with profit and revenue increasing by 27% and 3.4% respectively, driven by significant deal wins. The IT major forecasted a 2-4% growth for the quarter ending June, signaling a potential double-digit expansion, and won over $1.4 billion in deals including a potential $1 billion large deal. Despite rising attrition rates, Wipro plans to invest in talent, including hiring over 18,000 freshers, to sustain its growth momentum.

feed.title: Amazon plans furniture assembly service to catch Wayfair
feed.summary: Amazon.com Inc. is testing a new premium service offering immediate furniture and appliance assembly upon delivery, aiming to better compete with rivals like Wayfair, Best Buy, Home Depot, and Lowe’s. This service, anticipated to launch in Virginia and two other markets, would involve drivers assembling items, removing packaging, and handling immediate returns for dissatisfied customers. The initiative seeks to make delivery of large items more convenient and efficient, building on the increased online sales of furniture and housewares observed during the pandemic, although Amazon has struggled to match Wayfair's dominance in the online home-goods market.

feed.title: Oracle plans to bring 8,500 jobs, $1.2 billion campus to Nashville
feed.summary: Oracle Corporation plans to invest $1.2 billion in Nashville, bringing 8,500 jobs with an average salary of $110,000 to the city. This development would be the largest in Tennessee's history, involving significant public infrastructure contributions from Oracle without burdening taxpayers. The new campus will transform Nashville's East Bank, creating a major technology hub.

feed.title: Map: How Countries’ Stock Markets Stack Up on ESG Practices and Carbon Risk
feed.summary: This article examines global stock markets' performance on ESG practices and carbon risk, based on the Morningstar Sustainability Atlas. It highlights that European countries, especially the Netherlands, lead in sustainability scores, while emerging markets generally lag. The report also identifies countries with the lowest carbon risk, such as Switzerland and the U.S., contrasting them with high-risk nations like Russia due to their energy sector exposure.

feed.title: Zscaler Acquires Trustdome for Cloud Security Posture Management Boost -
feed.summary: Zscaler is acquiring Trustdome to enhance its cloud security posture management (CSPM) capabilities, aiming to help MSSPs, MSPs, and customers mitigate security risks across major cloud platforms. Trustdome's Cloud Infrastructure Entitlement Management (CIEM) platform will strengthen Zscaler's existing CSPM offerings by providing granular governance over data, applications, and services in public cloud environments. This acquisition comes amidst booming demand for CSPM solutions, with market revenues projected to reach $9 billion by 2026.

feed.title: Elon Musk’s SpaceX wins $2.9-billion NASA contract for moon spacecraft
feed.summary: NASA has awarded Elon Musk's SpaceX a $2.9 billion contract to develop a spacecraft for returning astronauts to the moon as early as 2024, bypassing competitors like Jeff Bezos' Blue Origin. This contract is part of NASA's Artemis program, aiming for a sustained lunar presence and eventually missions to Mars. SpaceX will need to conduct a test flight before human missions, deepening its collaboration with NASA.

feed.title: El behind the scenes Televisa-Univision
feed.summary: Televisa and Univision announced a multi-million dollar agreement to create the largest Spanish-language content company globally, focusing on the Spanish-speaking streaming market. The deal, valued at $4.8 billion for Televisa, involves international partners like Google and SoftBank, and made Televisa the largest shareholder with 45% of the shares. The announcement led to a significant 22-25% stock increase for Televisa and garnered positive public and governmental attention, highlighting the potential for content production in Spanish.

feed.title: An A.I.-Powered ETF? Look No Further than 'AMOM'
feed.summary: The QRAFT AI Enhanced U.S. Large Cap Momentum ETF (AMOM) uses artificial intelligence to identify U.S. large-cap stocks with strong momentum, aiming for long-term capital appreciation. It actively selects holdings based on their potential for maximum return, offering investors exposure to the momentum factor. Amazon was noted as its largest holding at the time of writing, highlighting the fund's diversified approach beyond just big tech.

feed.title: An A.I.-Powered ETF? Look No Further than 'AMOM'
feed.summary: The QRAFT AI Enhanced U.S. Large Cap Momentum ETF (AMOM) uses artificial intelligence to identify and invest in large-cap U.S. stocks exhibiting strong momentum. This actively managed ETF aims for long-term capital appreciation by selecting companies based on their momentum's potential impact on performance. Its top holding currently includes Amazon, highlighting its diversified sector approach.

feed.title: Microsoft to invest $1 bln in Malaysia to set up data centres - Malaysian PM
feed.summary: Microsoft Corporation announced a $1 billion investment over the next five years in Malaysia to establish its first "datacentre region" in the country. This initiative is part of a new partnership program with the Malaysian government and local companies, aiming to boost the nation's digital economy and provide digital skills training for up to a million Malaysians. The investment follows Malaysia's efforts to attract foreign direct investments after a significant decline last year.

feed.title: Are FYC Billboards DOA? Studios Pull Back on Outdoor Marketing Amid Pandemic
feed.summary: Studios significantly reduced their "For Your Consideration" (FYC) billboard advertising in Los Angeles during the early part of 2021 due to the pandemic, as Oscar voters were no longer commuting or attending in-person screenings. While out-of-home advertising for the cinema sector saw a 90 percent drop in 2020, the FYC market for billboards began recovering near the end of the awards season, with a projected return to full swing for Emmy season, partly driven by sustained investment from streaming companies. The pandemic also shifted billboard placement strategies, with advertisers becoming more open to suburban locations to target executives and talent in their homes.

feed.title: Clean Energy Fuels Partners with Amazon for Renewable Natural Gas
feed.summary: Clean Energy Fuels Corp. announced a fuel supply and investment agreement with Amazon for low and negative carbon renewable natural gas (RNG). The deal involves supplying RNG to Amazon facilities in 15 states at 27 existing Clean Energy fueling stations, with an expansion to 19 new or upgraded stations by the end of 2021. Amazon also received warrants to purchase up to 53.14 million shares of Clean Energy Fuels common stock, with initial vesting tied to the fuel supply agreement and remaining warrants contingent on future fuel purchases up to $500 million.

feed.title: Clean Energy Fuels inks supply deal with Amazon
feed.summary: Clean Energy Fuels has announced a new deal to supply renewable natural gas (RNG) to Amazon, expanding its network of RNG fueling stations across 15 states. The agreement also includes a warrant for Amazon to purchase a significant number of Clean Energy Fuels' common stock. This collaboration aims to provide an environmentally and economically sound solution for vehicle fuel, supporting efforts against climate change.

feed.title: DEADLINE ALERT for UAVS, IQDAX, IQDNX, and AQST: The Law
feed.summary: The Law Offices of Frank R. Cruz reminds investors of upcoming deadlines for class action lawsuits against AgEagle Aerial Systems Inc. (UAVS), Infinity Q Diversified Alpha Fund (IQDAX, IQDNX), and Aquestive Therapeutics, Inc. (AQST). Investors in these companies are encouraged to contact the law firm to discuss their legal rights, as the lawsuits allege materially false and/or misleading statements and failure to disclose adverse facts by the defendants. The deadlines for filing a lead plaintiff motion for these cases are April 27 and April 30, 2021.

feed.title: Netflix reports dramatic slowdown in subscribers
feed.summary: Netflix shares fell significantly after the company reported a large miss in subscriber numbers for Q1 2021, adding only 3.98 million paid net subscribers against an expectation of 6.2 million. The company attributed the slowdown to the "Covid-19 pull forward" in 2020 and production delays affecting its content slate, despite beating earnings per share and revenue estimates. Netflix expects content to pick up in the second half of the year and plans to spend over $17 billion on content, while also approving a $5 billion stock buyback program.

feed.title: Sunnyvale real estate mega-deal makes Google, big developer neighbors
feed.summary: Tishman Speyer completed a major real estate transaction in Sunnyvale, acquiring seven parcels from NetApp for $365 million, making them neighbors with Google. This deal includes four large office buildings and amenities, giving Tishman Speyer significant control in a prime Silicon Valley tech area. Google also owns adjacent properties, suggesting potential long-term redevelopment plans for both entities in northern Sunnyvale.

feed.title: Netflix’s Dominance Starts to Slow as Rivals Gain (Published 2021)
feed.summary: Netflix reported a slowdown in subscriber growth for the first quarter of 2021, adding only four million new customers compared to a forecast of six million. This modest growth, coupled with a decline in its share of global demand for original programming, indicates that competitors like Disney+, HBO Max, and Paramount+ are beginning to challenge Netflix's long-standing market dominance. The company anticipates adding even fewer subscribers in the current quarter.

feed.title: Amazon Taxation Becomes Sticking Point in Talks on Global Levies
feed.summary: Discussions on a global accord for taxing large tech companies' overseas profits are faltering due to disagreements spurred by Amazon's taxation. A U.S. proposal aims to tax around 100 top companies in countries where their users are, but Amazon's low margins could exempt it, and the U.S. objects to singling out any specific company.

feed.title: Pardon the Disruption: E-grocery and the evolving need for speed
feed.summary: The article discusses two contrasting models in online grocery delivery: Kroger's large-scale automated fulfillment centers designed for next-day delivery and the emerging ultrafast delivery services focused on 15-minute delivery from dark stores. It explores the evolving customer expectation regarding delivery speed, catalyzed by the pandemic, noting that while Kroger prioritizes efficiency and lower costs for large orders, many consumers are increasingly demanding faster, more localized on-demand services for smaller, impulse purchases. The piece suggests that the industry is grappling with whether to prioritize speed or efficiency as the e-grocery landscape continues to shift.

feed.title: Netflix tanks as subscriber growth cools
feed.summary: Netflix shares fell significantly after reporting only 4 million new subscribers in Q1 2021, a miss compared to its 6 million projection. The company attributed the slowdown to COVID-19 related production delays and faces growing competition from services like Disney+. Despite the subscriber miss, Netflix saw a 24% year-over-year revenue increase to $7.16 billion and expects growth to re-accelerate in the latter half of 2021 with new content.

feed.title: Formula 1's expansion in the U.S. is in motion, now it needs a star American driver
feed.summary: Formula 1 is expanding its presence in the U.S. with a new 10-year deal for a second race in Miami, complementing the existing U.S. Grand Prix in Austin. While Liberty Media's strategy for growth in the American market is taking shape, experts believe that securing a star American driver is the crucial next step to truly boost the sport's popularity and engagement in the nation. The article highlights potential American talent like Colton Herta and discusses F1's financial performance and viewership growth in the U.S.

feed.title: Marvell closes on $10 billion Inphi deal, expanding data center and 5G business
feed.summary: Marvell Technology has completed its $10 billion acquisition of Inphi after receiving approval from Chinese regulators, expanding its reach into data centers and 5G network infrastructure. CEO Matt Murphy noted the deal's overwhelmingly positive global customer support. The acquisition will enhance Marvell's growth in key markets such as 5G, cloud, and automotive, and Inphi's former CEO, Dr. Ford Tamer, has joined Marvell's board.

feed.title: The Need for Continuous and Dynamic Threat Modeling
feed.summary: This article emphasizes the importance of continuous and dynamic threat modeling in DevSecOps environments, moving beyond static, point-in-time assessments. It highlights how agile development and cloud deployments remove traditional checks and balances, necessitating real-time monitoring for architecture drift. The authors propose methods to transform operational views into dynamic threat models, contextualize security requirements, and continuously monitor for deviations from baseline security.

feed.title: Netflix Not-So-Secretly Wants to Be Disney, But Is It Placing the Right Bets?
feed.summary: Netflix is increasingly attempting to emulate Disney's strategy by focusing on key content verticals like animation, Hollywood blockbusters, and foreign shows. This shift comes as Netflix faces accelerating competition and a shrinking share of original digital demand, despite its large content budget. The article questions if these targeted investments will yield the same success and branding power as Disney's established intellectual property.

feed.title: Sour Jacks Unveils New Packaging and Varieties
feed.summary: The Promotion In Motion Companies, Inc., has updated the packaging graphics and expanded the flavor lineup for its Sour Jacks Sour Wedge Candies. The new additions include Original Sour Jacks Sour Wedges, a five-flavor mix, joining revamped watermelon, wildberry, and green apple varieties. These sour candies are available nationwide in 4 oz. and 5 oz. Peg Bags.

feed.title: Ball Corporation to Become Proud Sustainability Partner of Los Angeles Chargers
feed.summary: Ball Corporation announced a partnership with the Los Angeles Chargers to advance sustainability in sports and entertainment. This collaboration will focus on community engagement, increased use of aluminum beverage packaging, and game day activations to improve recycling education and processes. The partnership reinforces Ball's role as the official Infinitely Recyclable Aluminum Packaging Partner of SoFi Stadium and aims to implement real-world solutions to packaging waste.

feed.title: Get Growth With Large Cap Stability With The "VONG" ETF
feed.summary: The article highlights the Vanguard Russell 1000 Growth Index Fund ETF Shares (VONG) as a way for investors to achieve growth with large-cap stability. VONG tracks the Russell 1000 Growth Index, focusing on large-capitalization growth stocks in the U.S., performing well with a 60% increase in the past year, driven by major tech companies. It caters to investors seeking long-term growth opportunities and capital appreciation over dividends.

feed.title: Get Growth With Large Cap Stability With The "VONG" ETF
feed.summary: This article highlights the Vanguard Russell 1000 Growth Index Fund ETF Shares (VONG) as a means for investors to achieve growth with large-cap stability. VONG tracks the Russell 1000® Growth Index, focusing on large-capitalization growth stocks in the U.S. The ETF has shown significant growth over the past year, driven by prominent technology companies.

feed.title: Equal-Weight Still Holds Quality for Growth Investors
feed.summary: The article discusses how equal-weighted Nasdaq-100 ETFs like QQQE and QQEW can be beneficial for growth investors, particularly those looking to mitigate concentration risk in the cap-weighted Nasdaq-100 Index. By equal weighting, these funds reduce reliance on mega-cap firms and add a value factor, offering a more balanced exposure to the underlying companies. This approach helps lessen the impact of poor performance from individual large constituents.

feed.title: ‘That’s crazy!’: Tesla CEO Musk gives carbon removal $100m boost
feed.summary: Elon Musk has launched a $100 million prize competition to encourage the development of new carbon capture technologies. The initiative aims to address the pressing need for scalable and affordable methods to remove carbon dioxide from the atmosphere and oceans, thereby combating global warming. This four-year challenge, described as the largest incentive prize in history, seeks viable solutions capable of removing 1,000 tonnes of CO2 annually with long-term sequestration.

feed.title: Amazon Inks RNG Agreement, Considers Possible Stake in Clean Energy Fuels
feed.summary: Amazon Inc. has signed a five-year agreement with Clean Energy Fuels Corp. for renewable natural gas (RNG) and may acquire up to a 20% stake in the fuel supplier. The deal involves supplying RNG to Amazon's investment unit starting April 16, 2021, through 27 existing and 19 new fueling stations. This initiative aligns with Amazon's goal to become carbon-neutral by 2040 and signifies growing corporate interest in clean energy solutions.

feed.title: Oscars 2021: Netflix and Disney dominate in streaming’s biggest year
feed.summary: The 93rd Academy Awards saw Netflix and Disney dominate, reflecting the streaming-centric year. Netflix, despite losing Best Picture to Disney-owned Searchlight's “Nomadland,” secured seven awards, making it the top distributor. Disney, including wins from Searchlight and Pixar, also performed strongly with five trophies, solidifying streaming services' significant presence in the awards landscape.

feed.title: Popular clothing retailer TJ Maxx relocates, opens in Lincoln Plaza
feed.summary: Following the closure of the Greendale Mall, TJ Maxx has relocated and opened a new store in Lincoln Plaza, Worcester. The new location, which also includes a HomeGoods, fills a void left by Barnes & Noble and is part of TJX Companies Inc.

feed.title: SiriusXM acquires Roman Mars’ 99% Invisible and a bigger stake in the podcasting world
feed.summary: SiriusXM has acquired Roman Mars' podcast company 99% Invisible Inc., integrating Mars and his team under its Stitcher brand. This acquisition allows Mars to focus on content creation while benefiting from SiriusXM's resources and distribution across platforms like Stitcher, Pandora, and SiriusXM apps. The move highlights the ongoing consolidation in the podcasting industry as larger tech companies vie for top talent and content.

feed.title: How managed multi-cloud services help create an industrialized approach
feed.summary: This article discusses how managed multi-cloud services enable enterprises to adopt an industrialized approach to IT, ensuring agility and effective management of both cloud and non-cloud resources. It highlights how these services help meet business imperatives, foster innovation through CoEs and advanced technologies, and optimize IT consumption. Wipro's BoundaryLess Enterprise (BLE) platform is presented as a solution for accelerating cloud transformation and aligning business objectives with IT resource consumption.

feed.title: First Person: Litigator David Boies On Antitrust, Tech And The Government V. Microsoft
feed.summary: Litigator David Boies discusses his experience representing the government in the landmark antitrust case against Microsoft in the late 1990s. He explains how Microsoft's monopolistic practices were challenged and reflects on the lessons learned from that case, particularly concerning the applicability of antitrust laws to technology companies. Boies also touches on the challenges of applying these regulations to modern tech giants like Amazon, emphasizing the need for fact-specific analysis.

feed.title: Chart of the day: Fuel cell industry profits
feed.summary: Despite a significant boost in stock prices for public fuel cell companies in 2020 due to the "hydrogen funding wave", their profits did not follow suit. The article highlights persistent financial losses and slow growth in the sector, citing companies like Ballard Power and Plug Power, and discusses the technological challenges and ongoing R&D efforts to improve fuel cell viability.

feed.title: Experian’s Credit Freeze Security is Still a Joke
feed.summary: This article criticizes Experian's credit freeze security, highlighting that it remains vulnerable to identity theft despite being exposed in 2017. It details how Experian's "request your PIN" feature is easily exploited using publicly available information and how the company pushes paid "CreditLock" services for enhanced security that should be standard with a free freeze. The author argues that Experian prioritizes profit over consumer data protection, mirroring similar security flaws found in Equifax and TransUnion.

feed.title: Tesla beats quarterly revenue expectations on robust demand
feed.summary: Tesla Inc. reported its seventh consecutive quarter of profit, exceeding revenue expectations due to strong demand for its Model 3 sedan. The company reiterated its multi-year outlook for 50% growth in deliveries and is expanding operations across three continents with new factories in Texas and Berlin. Despite a positive quarter, Tesla faces increased competition from both established automakers and new EV startups.

feed.title: Unilever snaps up US supplements firm Onnit
feed.summary: Unilever has acquired Onnit, a US-based holistic wellness and lifestyle company specializing in supplements, including the nootropic Alpha Brain, and a range of packaged food and sports nutrition products. This acquisition aligns with Unilever's strategy to expand its portfolio in innovative wellness and supplement brands, following prior purchases of SmartyPants Vitamins and Olly Nutrition. Onnit will continue its operations in Austin, Texas, with its founder Aubrey Marcus remaining a brand ambassador.

feed.title: FedEx is the engine of e-commerce during COVID-19
feed.summary: FedEx has served as a crucial engine for e-commerce during the COVID-19 pandemic, leveraging Adobe Experience Cloud solutions to meet surging customer demands. The company rapidly expanded capacity and adapted its digital platforms to handle increased online shopping, personalizing experiences for businesses and consumers alike. FedEx's long-term planning and agility, along with tools like FedEx Delivery Manager, enabled them to navigate unprecedented growth and a challenging holiday season.

feed.title: Looking for an ETF to Hold for the Long-Term?
feed.summary: The article suggests the Schwab U.S. Large-Cap Growth ETF (SCHG) as a long-term investment option, highlighting its strong performance and holdings in major growth companies. It emphasizes the potential for significant long-term returns through consistent investing in such ETFs, despite the allure of instant gratification.

feed.title: Arm Puts Some Muscle Into Future Neoverse Server CPU Designs
feed.summary: Arm is unveiling details about its future Neoverse V1 ("Zeus") and N2 ("Perseus") server CPU designs, aimed at the datacenter and edge. The V1 core is designed for high-performance computing and enterprise workloads, offering significantly better single-threaded integer performance than its predecessor, while the N2 core optimizes for performance per dollar and watt, targeting mainstream infrastructure servers. These new designs, based on the upcoming Armv9-A architecture, highlight Arm's continued push into the server market with tailored solutions.

feed.title: Form 8-K IDEX CORP /DE/ For: Apr 27
feed.summary: IDEX Corporation reported strong first-quarter 2021 financial results, with record orders and sales up 10% overall and 6% organically. The company raised its full-year adjusted EPS guidance to $6.05-$6.20 and announced the acquisition of AirTech Group, Inc. These results reflect improving market conditions and effective capital deployment strategies.

feed.title: Spotify forecasts weak quarter as subscriber growth falters
feed.summary: Spotify Technology SA has forecast second-quarter paid subscribers below Wall Street estimates, despite its first-quarter results beating expectations due to growth in countries like the United States and India. The company faces increasing competition from rivals such as Apple Music and Amazon Music, with Apple also launching a new paid subscription platform challenging Spotify's podcast ambitions. Spotify anticipates total premium subscribers between 162 million and 166 million for Q2, while analysts expected 166.1 million.

feed.title: Apple sales smash expectations on surging demand for devices
feed.summary: Apple Inc. reported fiscal second-quarter sales of $89.6 billion, significantly exceeding Wall Street expectations, driven by strong demand for the 5G iPhone 12 line, iPads, and Mac computers as people continued working and learning from home. The company's iPad and Mac sales nearly doubled, demonstrating its control over the supply chain and its profitability during the pandemic. Apple also increased its dividend by 7% and expanded its share buyback program by an additional $90 billion.

feed.title: A message from Darcie Henry, Amazon VP of People eXperience and Technology, Worldwide Consumer
feed.summary: Amazon is increasing pay for more than 500,000 employees in Customer Fulfillment, Delivery, Package Sortation, and Specialty Fulfillment teams, with raises between 50 cents and $3 an hour, totaling over $1 billion in incremental pay. This builds on an industry-leading starting wage of at least $15 an hour and an additional $2.5 billion invested in bonuses and incentives last year. These positions include a comprehensive benefits package and opportunities for career advancement in a safe and inclusive environment.

feed.title: SOPREMA Welcomes Jeff Guritza as Director of Marketing
feed.summary: SOPREMA Inc. has appointed Jeff Guritza as its new director of marketing, bringing over 25 years of global industrial marketing experience from prominent companies. Guritza will oversee all marketing initiatives, focusing on demand generation and enhancing the customer journey. This strategic hire is aimed at expanding SOPREMA's market visibility and proactively generating demand for its diverse product lines during a period of growth.

feed.title: How to get started reselling common products on EBay and other sites
feed.summary: During the pandemic, many people began reselling common consumer products on sites like EBay due to financial concerns or excess items. This article provides a guide for aspiring resellers, addressing common questions about legality, sourcing products, and startup costs. It also highlights the increase in new sellers on platforms like EBay, which has partnered with liquidators to provide inventory.

feed.title: Morgan Stanley, Veteran Broker Dinged $800k Over Amazon Short
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley and veteran broker Jacob G. Shapira have been ordered to pay nearly $800,000 in damages to Israeli company Mikedmat Dena Inc. following unsuitable and unauthorized trades, including shorting Amazon stock. The FINRA arbitration panel found Shapira liable for trades made between 2015 and 2020. Shapira has a history of customer disputes, with several prior complaints alleging unsuitable and unauthorized trading.

feed.title: Uber Rides App to Prompt Food-Delivery Orders
feed.summary: Uber is integrating its rides and food-delivery businesses further by adding features to its app. The company will prompt passengers requesting rides from airports about food delivery options via Uber Eats, and an Uber One feature will allow users to pick up meals while en route. This strategy aims to drive business to Uber Eats as the rides sector recovers from the pandemic and food delivery demand is expected to normalize.

feed.title: Greenlight waves in Wells Fargo to raise $260m
feed.summary: Greenlight Financial Technology, a US-based provider of banking platforms for children, successfully raised $260 million in a Series D funding round, valuing the company at $2.3 billion. Wells Fargo's Strategic Capital unit was among the investors, with Andreessen Horowitz leading the round. This funding will be used to accelerate product development, expand geographically, and invest in distribution partnerships for Greenlight's money management platform and debit card.

feed.title: Walt Disney Company confirms AWS as preferred cloud partner for Disney+ streaming service
feed.summary: The Walt Disney Company has designated Amazon Web Services (AWS) as its preferred cloud partner for the Disney+ streaming service, which has garnered over 100 million subscribers globally. Disney+ leveraged AWS for its rapid international expansion and will further integrate AWS's machine learning, database, storage, and analytics services. This partnership highlights AWS's broader strategy to offer sector-specific cloud services for the media and entertainment industry.

feed.title: The Walt Disney Company partners AWS for Disney+ expansion
feed.summary: The Walt Disney Company has partnered with Amazon Web Services (AWS) for the global rollout and expansion of its Disney+ streaming service. AWS has been Disney's preferred public cloud infrastructure provider, supporting Disney+'s growth to over 100 million subscribers in 59 countries. This expanded partnership will see Disney+ leverage more than 50 AWS technologies, including machine learning, database, storage, and analytics, to enhance its content delivery and platform efficacy.

feed.title: Consumer spending, streaming propel Amazon to second straight quarter of more than $100B in revenue
feed.summary: Amazon reported quarterly sales of $108.5 billion, marking its second consecutive quarter exceeding $100 billion in revenue, driven by strong consumer spending and increased streaming by its 200 million Prime members. The company's profit more than tripled, leading to an all-time high in stock price and plans for a Prime Day sale in June. Amazon also announced pay raises for over 500,000 workers, effective next month.

feed.title: Disney+ chooses Amazon Web Services for global expansion
feed.summary: The Walt Disney Company has selected Amazon Web Services (AWS) as its preferred public cloud infrastructure provider for the global expansion of Disney+. Leveraging AWS's fault-tolerant and highly performant infrastructure, Disney+ has rapidly grown to over 100 million subscribers in 59 countries. The streaming service utilizes more than 50 AWS technologies, including machine learning, databases, and analytics, to handle content ingestion, manage customer actions, and ensure high-quality video delivery.

feed.title: I'm Not Turning Off App Tracking On My iPhone, Because I’m A Total Cuck
feed.summary: The author humorously explains her decision to not disable app tracking on her iPhone, despite Apple's new privacy feature allowing users to opt out. She describes herself as a "datacuck" who enjoys personalized ads and wants to see "how far this goes" when it comes to data collection, contrasting her personal choice with the recommended smart privacy practices. The article satirically delves into the perceived benefits of targeted advertising while acknowledging the serious implications of data privacy and Facebook's track record.

feed.title: Building a Resilient E-Commerce Business with Arrow Electronics
feed.summary: This article discusses how Arrow Electronics, a global distributor, is building a resilient e-commerce business in response to market disruptions and the impact of COVID-19. It details how the company adopted SAP Commerce Cloud and restructured operations to improve customer experience and achieve business agility through digital transformation. The article highlights the importance of adapting to B2B buyer demands and leveraging technology to overcome supply chain challenges.

feed.title: El equipo legal detrás de la fusión de Televisa y Univisión
feed.summary: The article details the legal teams involved in the merger of Televisa and Univision, outlining the firms that advised both companies, as well as investors like SoftBank and The Raine Group. This historic merger aims to create the largest Spanish-language multimedia company globally, combining content and assets to compete with streaming platforms. The financial structure of the deal, including Univision's payment to Televisa and funding from various investors, is also highlighted.

feed.title: AT&T shareholders vote against approving executive compensation
feed.summary: AT&T shareholders have voted against approving executive compensation during the company's annual meeting, with only 49% of votes cast in favor. This advisory vote comes as executive pay packages have grown, and highlights shareholder concerns, particularly regarding the more than $52 million compensation for WarnerMedia CEO Jason Kilar, which was double that of AT&T CEO John Stankey. AT&T's board acknowledged the vote and stated they would consider it to ensure their compensation approach aligns with company principles.

feed.title: Warren Buffett’s Berkshire Hathaway rebounds from pandemic with $11.7 billion profit
feed.summary: Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway announced a net profit of $11.7 billion in the first quarter of 2021, recovering from a significant loss in early 2020 due to the pandemic. The conglomerate, which owns diverse businesses, saw strong revenue growth as the economy reopened. Despite a massive cash pile of $145.4 billion, Berkshire Hathaway continued significant stock buybacks rather than pursuing large acquisitions, even as its stock performance has lagged the broader market in recent years.

feed.title: Bracing for a record pool chlorine shortage in summer 2021
feed.summary: Pool owners are facing a record chlorine shortage in summer 2021 due to a pandemic-driven increase in demand and a fire at a major chemical plant. This has led to steep price increases for chlorine tablets, with some retailers advising customers to stock up now or consider alternative sanitization methods like saltwater systems or liquid chlorine. The shortage is impacting corporate earnings and is expected to continue through the peak pool season.

feed.title: 2021 Tech Titans
feed.summary: This article highlights the 2021 Tech Titans in Washington D.C.'s digital economy, recognizing over 170 influential leaders, innovators, and officials who demonstrated resilience and growth despite the pandemic. It showcases entrepreneurs who launched new companies, secured significant funding, and adapted their businesses, along with key figures in finance, cybersecurity, government, and policy. The piece also acknowledges leaders in the satellite, telecommunications, and defense industries who are shaping the region's tech landscape.

feed.title: Kroger delivery takes to the skies with drone pilot
feed.summary: Kroger is launching a drone delivery pilot program this spring, starting with test flights near a Kroger Marketplace in Centerville, Ohio, and expanding to a Ralphs location in California in the summer. The program will utilize autonomous drones from Drone Express to deliver groceries weighing up to 5 pounds within 15 minutes, directly to consumers' locations within flight range. This initiative follows similar efforts by Amazon and Walmart, as Kroger aims to enhance its online fulfillment ecosystem and compete in the rapidly evolving grocery delivery market.

feed.title: Google to power Virginia data centers with 24/7 clean energy from AES
feed.summary: AES has signed a 10-year agreement to exclusively supply 24/7 clean energy to three Google data centers in Virginia, utilizing a 500-MW portfolio of resources. This partnership aims to decarbonize Google's operations in the state and sets a precedent for other companies seeking to eliminate carbon from their energy supply. The AES portfolio will require approximately $600 million for its clean energy projects.

feed.title: Boeing is giving Virginia Tech $50 million to help launch new campus
feed.summary: Boeing has pledged a $50 million multiyear donation to Virginia Tech, marking the largest corporate gift in the university's history. This funding is intended to support financial aid and other initiatives for Virginia Tech's new graduate technology campus in Northern Virginia, with a focus on building and diversifying the student body. The investment highlights growing corporate interest in educational projects near major business hubs, such as Amazon's new headquarters.

feed.title: AVODs double down on original programming, content studios at NewFronts
feed.summary: Several ad-supported video on demand (AVOD) services, including Tubi, IMDb TV, Crackle Plus, and fuboTV, announced significant investment in original programming and content studios at the IAB NewFronts. This move comes as AVODs experience increased viewership and advertiser interest, driven by changing consumer behavior during the pandemic and a desire for alternatives to subscription-based services.
