Companies are leveraging these new offerings and enhanced targeting capabilities to attract both viewers and advertisers.

feed.title: JD Logistics approved for IPO; spinoff could raise $4B
feed.summary: JD Logistics, the logistics arm of Chinese e-commerce giant JD.com, has received approval for an initial public offering (IPO) on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange, potentially raising up to $4 billion. This follows JD Health International's successful IPO. Despite significant investments in automation and technology leading to a net loss in 2020, the company aims for profitability through customer expansion, leveraging its extensive warehouse network and advanced logistics capabilities to support JD.com's growth in China's fragmented retail market.

feed.title: Televisa y TV Azteca se recuperan en publicidad, pero toca impulsar el contenido
feed.summary: Televisa and TV Azteca are seeing a recovery in advertising revenue after a challenging 2020, with Televisa's ad income up 28.1% and TV Azteca's up 8% in Q1 2021. However, despite increased viewership during the COVID-19 pandemic, both companies face the challenge of migrating advertising investment to digital platforms and the need to innovate content to retain audiences and compete with streaming services. Experts emphasize that investing in new, high-quality content is crucial for the future of open television.

feed.title: Jessica Alba’s Honest Co. IPO soars on $1.4 billion valuation
feed.summary: Jessica Alba's The Honest Company Inc. made its public debut, with its IPO raising over $412 million and valuing the company at $1.4 billion. The stock, trading under the ticker HNST, saw a significant pop of over 40% on its first day. The wellness brand, founded in 2011, offers clean beauty, baby products, and household items, and has expanded its retail presence.

feed.title: Drivers don't work for Amazon but company has lots of rules for them
feed.summary: Amazon exerts significant control over its Delivery Service Partners (DSPs) and their drivers, despite not directly employing them, dictating everything from personal grooming to social media posts. This "fissured" labor model helps Amazon avoid costs and liabilities, but legal experts warn it could lead to "joint employer" or "vicarious liability" claims, especially under closer scrutiny in the Biden administration. The company faces ongoing lawsuits regarding wage theft and accountability for accidents, highlighting the controversial nature of its delivery operations.

feed.title: Amazon Work Rules Govern Tweets, Body Odor of Contract Drivers
feed.summary: Amazon dictates extensive rules for its contract delivery drivers, covering aspects from personal grooming and social media posts to mandatory training and drug testing. These detailed policies, despite drivers being employed by independent partners, raise questions about potential legal and regulatory challenges given the level of control Amazon exerts over these workers. The company's efforts to standardize its vast delivery network could lead to scrutiny over employment classifications.

feed.title: Strategy Analytics: Amazon's Ring Claimed Top Spot in Global Home Security Camera Market in 2020
feed.summary: In 2020, Amazon's Ring successfully doubled its market share and became the leading smart home security camera brand globally, surpassing Arlo and Wyze, according to Strategy Analytics. This growth was attributed to an aggressive expansion of its camera portfolio, particularly spotlight-equipped outdoor and indoor cameras, capitalizing on the brand's strong reputation from its video doorbells. The report also highlights the other top five brands as Arlo, Wyze, Google’s Nest, and Alarm.com, noting a total of 42.5 million cameras shipped to consumers in 2020 by all brands combined.

feed.title: The Honest Company Founder Jessica Alba Rings The Bell
feed.summary: Lightspeed Venture Partners celebrates The Honest Company's IPO, reflecting on its journey from a mission-driven startup founded by Jessica Alba in 2011 to a public company. The article highlights the company's initial rapid growth, subsequent challenges, and its return to profitability under CEO Nick Vlahos's leadership. Lightspeed expresses pride in supporting Honest Company's commitment to clean and conscious innovation.

feed.title: Five Guys, Several New Businesses Coming To North Haven: Freda
feed.summary: North Haven First Selectman Michael Freda announced that several new businesses, including a Five Guys restaurant, a Starbucks with a drive-thru, and an O’Reilly’s Auto Parts Store, are coming to the town. Additionally, a new Mexican restaurant called Torito, a Hampton Inn, Panera Bread, and Jersey Mike’s Subs are planned for Universal Drive and other locations. These developments are part of an effort to grow the town's grand list and enhance consumer services.

feed.title: Jessica Alba's Honest Co. surges in Nasdaq debut
feed.summary: Jessica Alba's eco-friendly personal care brand, Honest Co. (HNST), saw its shares surge 44% in its Nasdaq debut, closing at $23 per share and giving the company a market value of about $2.1 billion. CEO Nick Vlahos viewed the successful IPO as validation of the company's mission-driven strategy focused on clean, sustainable, and socially responsible products. Despite its growth in sales to over $300 million in 2020 and narrowing net losses, the company has not yet turned a profit and has previously faced controversial lawsuits regarding its product labeling.

feed.title: Iron Mountain joins The Climate Pledge
feed.summary: Iron Mountain has joined The Climate Pledge, committing to achieve net-zero carbon emissions by 2040, a decade ahead of the Paris Agreement's goal. As an information and asset management business, Iron Mountain has already significantly reduced its greenhouse gas emissions and aims for 100% clean power generation for its data centers. This move signifies its dedication to environmental impact reduction and aligns with over 100 other companies in the pledge.

feed.title: Wealth Advisors Value Human Interaction and Technology
feed.summary: Wealth advisors are navigating evolving client expectations, particularly from younger generations who prioritize digital experiences. A FactSet survey highlights that while technology adoption is crucial for efficiency and managing relationships, human interaction remains vital for discussing client goals and market conditions. Advisors are seeking consolidated, intuitive, and integrated platforms to enhance their effectiveness and proactively engage with clients.

feed.title: Transformers Kingdom Airazor Concept Art by Ken Christiansen
feed.summary: Ken Christiansen has shared new concept art for the Transformers Kingdom series, specifically featuring Airazor. The artwork includes notes from Christiansen and was commissioned in partnership with Hasbro, Inc. The original art drawings are now available on Hasbro Pulse.

feed.title: Amazon Sidewalk is About to Strengthen the Finding Power of Your Tiles
feed.summary: This short article announces that Amazon Sidewalk will enhance the tracking capabilities of Tile devices. It suggests that users will benefit from a stronger finding network for their Tiles.

feed.title: NRG Energy names Houston sole HQ, hires CFO from 'Amazon of South Korea'
feed.summary: NRG Energy has officially designated Houston as its sole headquarters, a shift from its previous dual-headquarters structure shared with Princeton, New Jersey, since 2012. Concurrently, the company has announced the hiring of a new Chief Financial Officer from Coupang, an e-commerce company dubbed the "Amazon of South Korea." This move follows NRG's acquisition of Houston-based GenOn and marks a significant step in centralizing its operations in Texas.

feed.title: Office Depot parent plans to break up its retail and B2B businesses
feed.summary: Office Depot's parent company plans to separate its retail (ODP) and business-to-business (NewCo) units into two independent, publicly traded companies. This move aims to maximize the strategic focus and financial flexibility of each entity, as their trajectories have diverged over the years, further highlighted by the pandemic's impact on their respective sales. The separation is expected to be completed in the first half of 2022, following similar actions by Staples' parent company.

feed.title: 3 Reasons Pinduoduo Stock Fell by More Than 30% in 2021
feed.summary: Pinduoduo's stock fell by over 30% in 2021 due to three main factors: the surprising departure of its founder and chairman Zheng Huang, increased regulatory scrutiny on Chinese tech companies, and a broader market shift away from high-growth tech stocks towards "reopening" plays. Despite these short-term challenges, the company's long-term prospects remain strong, with plans to expand customer spending on its platform and grow its agriculture-related gross merchandise value significantly by 2025. New CEO Lei Chen, part of the original team, is expected to continue driving the company's growth.

feed.title: Help With Vaccination Push Comes From Unexpected Businesses (Published 2021)
feed.summary: Major companies like Microsoft, Starbucks, and Amazon are assisting the government's COVID-19 vaccination efforts by offering logistical support, physical locations, and operational expertise. These companies, despite not being directly in healthcare, are leveraging their vast resources, national footprints, and distribution networks to help with the rollout. This private sector involvement aims to boost the vaccination drive and could enhance the companies' public image and bottom line.

feed.title: Sanctions-as-a-Service: US Regulators Escalate Sanctions Enforcement Priorities Into the Cloud with SAP Settlement
feed.summary: US regulators announced a global resolution with SAP SE following its voluntary disclosure of providing software and cloud-based services to users in Iran, violating export and sanctions regulations. This settlement, involving the Departments of Justice, Commerce, and Treasury, highlights the US government's increased focus on sanctions compliance in the tech sector, particularly regarding cloud-based services and the need for robust screening and access controls. SAP agreed to pay over $8 million in penalties and invested over $27 million in enhancing its compliance programs.

feed.title: Sectors Disrupted: Breaking E-commerce Out of the Box
feed.summary: The article discusses investment opportunities in e-commerce, presenting it as an alternative to traditional consumer discretionary or technology investments. It highlights ProShares ETFs like EMTY, CLIX, and ONLN, which allow investors to capitalize on the growth of online retail and the decline of brick-and-mortar stores. A webcast is announced where experts will further discuss these themes and strategies.

feed.title: Metro East distribution center to undergo $7M expansion
feed.summary: TriStar Properties is investing $7 million to expand a distribution center in Edwardsville, Illinois, leased by Phillips 66. The project will add 60,000 square feet to the existing 102,500-square-foot facility within the Gateway Commerce Center. This expansion is expected to increase the building's value to approximately $18 million once completed in October.

feed.title: Amazon borrows $18.5 billion it doesn’t need in new debt sale
feed.summary: Amazon.com Inc. issued $18.5 billion in new debt, its largest bond sale ever, despite holding $73 billion in cash and cash equivalents. The company is taking advantage of historically low borrowing costs to refinance debt, buy back stock, and fund general corporate purposes, including potential acquisitions and green projects. This move highlights a trend among U.S. investment-grade firms to secure cheap financing for growth and strategic initiatives as the economy rebounds.

feed.title: Dealmaker: JBG Smith, J.P. Morgan Global Alternatives Form Joint Venture for Potomac Yard Redevelopment
feed.summary: JBG Smith and J.P. Morgan Global Alternatives have formed a joint venture to redevelop nearly two million square feet of mixed-use space in Potomac Yard, Alexandria, VA, near Amazon's future HQ2 and Virginia Tech's Innovation Campus. JBG Smith will contribute land and serve as developer, property manager, and leasing agent, expanding its ownership in National Landing. The project includes two multifamily buildings set to begin construction within 12 months, with the entire site accessible via the new Potomac Yard Metro station.

feed.title: MySize debuts virtual fit avatar tool
feed.summary: MySize, an e-commerce measurement tech startup, has launched a new avatar feature that allows online shoppers to virtually try on clothes. This tool aims to reduce returns, which have become more common and costly with the surge in e-commerce, by helping customers visualize how garments will fit without sharing personal photos. The company hopes this "gamification" will enhance the interactive shopping experience and build customer loyalty.

feed.title: GaN IC maker Navitas to go public
feed.summary: Navitas Semiconductor, a GaN power IC company, is set to go public by merging with Live Oak Acquisition Corp II, a special purpose acquisition company. The transaction values the combined entity at $1.4 billion, enabling Navitas to expand its reach from mobile and consumer markets into more power-intensive applications. Navitas has already shipped integrated circuits to major companies such as Dell, LG, and Amazon.

feed.title: AWS, HPE, Other IT Vendors Back $50B Call For U.S. Chip Production
feed.summary: A coalition including major IT vendors like AWS, HPE, Apple, and Microsoft is advocating for $50 billion in U.S. government funding for domestic semiconductor manufacturing and research. This initiative, part of the CHIPS for America Act, aims to address the decline in U.S. chip production share and bolster supply chain resiliency. While supporting the funding, the group advised against government intervention in the current chip shortage and urged careful allocation of funds to benefit the entire industry rather than individual companies.

feed.title: Wheels Up Taps Former Amazon and Airbnb Executive Vinayak Hegde as Chief Marketplace Officer
feed.summary: Wheels Up, a leading private aviation brand, has appointed Vinayak Hegde, formerly of Amazon and Airbnb, as its Chief Marketplace Officer. In this role, Hegde will be responsible for the strategy and execution of Wheels Up's marketplace initiatives, including revenue, product, technology, business strategy, sales, and member experience. His expertise in scaling consumer internet offerings is expected to help Wheels Up expand its private aviation platform and increase accessibility.

feed.title: Wealthfront Adds Marketing Powerhouse Jaleh Bisharat to Board of Directors
feed.summary: Wealthfront announced that marketing executive and entrepreneur Jaleh Bisharat has joined its Board of Directors. Bisharat brings over 30 years of experience with consumer brands like Amazon, Eventbrite, OpenTable, and Upwork, and is also the co-founder and CEO of NakedPoppy. Her expertise will be instrumental in expanding Wealthfront's reach and supporting its technology-driven approach to financial services.

feed.title: Streaming Giants Are Spending Billions Overseas. Here Comes the Pushback.
feed.summary: U.S.-based streaming services like Netflix, Amazon, Disney+, and HBO Max are investing billions in overseas markets to produce local-language content, leading to a boom for local talent and increased competition. However, this global expansion is also generating pushback from established international broadcasters, local lawmakers, and producers who are raising concerns about equal pay and content ownership. This development occurs as Disney+ has boosted its parent company's stock, highlighting the significant impact of streaming in the entertainment industry.

feed.title: Netgear WN3000RP vs Linksys RE6500: which Wi-Fi extender is better?
feed.summary: This article compares two Wi-Fi extenders, the Netgear WN3000RP and the Linksys RE6500, to help consumers choose the best option for their needs and budget. It highlights their different features, target markets, and benefits, noting that the Netgear is a more affordable entry-level option, while the Linksys is a high-end device with superior range and speed. The piece concludes by recommending the Netgear for basic signal extension and the Linksys for more demanding users seeking advanced capabilities.

feed.title: To All The Things Lana Condor Has Loved Before, Including Her Favorite Lip Balm
feed.summary: Actress and producer Lana Condor discusses her collaboration with Vera Bradley for their new Recycled Cotton collection, including a limited-edition backpack she designed. She shares insights into her favorite everyday items, from her go-to lip balm and best jeans to unexpected product discoveries and sentimental gifts. The article highlights her personal style and recommendations.

feed.title: Safaricom in Talks With U.S. Giant Amazon on Mobile Payments
feed.summary: Safaricom Plc, Kenya's largest company, is in discussions with Amazon.com Inc. to integrate its M-Pesa mobile-money service onto Amazon's e-commerce platform. This move is part of Safaricom's strategy to expand its global reach and recover from its first profit decline in a decade, viewing fintech as a crucial driver for sales. The talks aim to enable M-Pesa payments for online retail purchases.

feed.title: JPMorgan Is America’s Largest Company In 2021, But Amazon Is Rising In The Ranks
feed.summary: Despite a pandemic, America's largest companies grew significantly in 2021, with JPMorgan Chase maintaining its position as the largest for the third consecutive year. Meanwhile, Amazon climbed into the top five, reflecting the growing importance of e-commerce, while traditional giants like AT&T and ExxonMobil saw significant drops in rankings. This year's Global 2000 ranking illustrates the resilience and shifts within the U.S. corporate landscape amidst economic volatility.

feed.title: Gunmaker Troy moving to Clarksville with 75 jobs, leaving Massachusetts over ‘changing climate’
feed.summary: Firearms manufacturer Troy Industries is relocating its headquarters and manufacturing operations from West Springfield, Massachusetts, to Clarksville, Tennessee. This move, which represents a $7.2 million investment and 75 new jobs, is largely due to the "changing climate" for gunmakers in Massachusetts. The company is actively recruiting, focusing on veterans, women, and minorities, and anticipates contributing significantly to Clarksville's economic growth.

feed.title: NHL, Amazon Web Services to debut advanced stats during playoffs
feed.summary: The NHL and Amazon Web Services (AWS) announced the debut of two new advanced analytics, "shot analytics" and "save analytics," during the 2021 Stanley Cup Playoffs. These stats will be displayed as on-screen graphics and data visualizations, providing fans with deeper insights into player and team performance. Leveraging historical NHL data and real-time game activity, these analytics aim to enhance the broadcast viewing experience and offer new perspectives for fans, players, coaches, and broadcasters.

feed.title: 2021 Ford F-150 and Mustang Mach-E Owners: New Features Coming Your Way
feed.summary: Ford is rolling out a series of Power-Up software updates for its 2021 F-150 and Mustang Mach-E vehicles equipped with Sync 4. These over-the-air updates include bug fixes for issues like zone lighting and Android Auto connectivity, as well as enhancements to features like Apple CarPlay display and the native navigation system. New functionalities, such as the BlueCruise hands-free driving software and a drawing/gaming feature called Sketch or Notes for the Mach-E, are also being introduced, along with enhanced Amazon Alexa integration.

feed.title: Alexa® Functionality Now Available for FirstEnergy Utility Customers
feed.summary: FirstEnergy utility customers can now use Amazon's Alexa-enabled devices to access information about their electric bills. This new functionality provides details such as billing notifications, due dates, payment confirmations, and monthly/yearly billing comparisons at no additional cost. The company plans to add more features like kilowatt-hour usage comparisons and bill payment capabilities in the future.

feed.title: XPO Logistics Increases Hiring Activity in North America to Meet Growing Logistics Demand
feed.summary: XPO Logistics is recruiting for over 4,000 jobs across North America to meet the growing demand in its logistics business, driven by automation, outsourcing, and e-commerce. This hiring initiative precedes the planned spin-off of its logistics segment into GXO Logistics, Inc. The company is seeking material handlers, inventory specialists, and robotic maintenance technicians, offering competitive pay and benefits.

feed.title: Transformers Kingdom Dinobot Concept Art by Ken Christiansen
feed.summary: Ken Christiansen has revealed new concept art for the Transformers Kingdom Dinobot figure. The art includes detailed drawings of the Dinobot in its robot mode, commissioned in partnership with Hasbro, Inc. These designs are now available for viewing on Hasbro Pulse.
feed.banner_image: https://i.tformers.com/g/albums/41446/Transformers Kingdom Dinobot Concept Art by Ken Christiansen (1).jpg

feed.title: Polaris Warranty Program Faced Significant Challenges, 13 May 2021
feed.summary: In 2012, Polaris faced a 34% premium rate increase on its Polaris Protection Plan due to an actuarial review by its third-party administrator. After, Inc. conducted an independent analysis, identifying significant errors in the carrier's methodology, particularly in how premiums were earned versus claims incurred. By presenting these findings, After, Inc. helped Polaris avoid the rate increase and subsequently moved Polaris to a fully in-house warranty company, saving millions and ensuring program stability.

feed.title: Is TiVo done with streaming dongles? Not so fast
feed.summary: Despite recent reports suggesting TiVo might be abandoning its streaming dongle business, a company spokesperson states that TiVo will continue to produce new Android TV dongles and improve the existing Stream 4K. The move comes as TiVo's parent company, Xperi, also explores embedding its software directly into smart TVs. The article highlights the potential of TiVo's streaming solution for cord-cutters and the competitive landscape of both streaming devices and smart TV platforms.

feed.title: Polen Focus Growth Fund Purchased Amazon.com (AMZN), Should You Do the Same?
feed.summary: Polen Capital's "Polen Focus Growth" fund purchased Amazon.com (AMZN) in February 2021, making it 5% of their portfolio, based on increased visibility into the company's free cash flow margins and future earnings growth from AWS and Advertising. The fund, which returned 1.81% in Q1 2021, highlighted Amazon's evolving profit drivers from e-commerce to higher-margin services. Despite past concerns about Amazon's fit with their guardrails, Polen Capital now believes these high-margin segments could drive over 30% annual earnings growth for the next five years.

feed.title: Amdocs Acquires Sourced to Further Boost Communications Industry’s Move to the Cloud
feed.summary: Amdocs has acquired Sourced Group, a leading global technology consultancy, to enhance its capabilities in helping the communications industry transition to the cloud. This acquisition expands Amdocs’ portfolio of cloud-native products and services, leveraging Sourced’s decade of experience in large-scale cloud transformations for high-end enterprises. The deal, valued at approximately $75 million in cash, is expected to be neutral to Amdocs' non-GAAP diluted earnings per share for fiscal year 2021.

feed.title: Elasticsearch on AWS: Deploying Your First Managed Cluster
feed.summary: This article explains how to deploy a managed Elasticsearch cluster on Amazon Web Services (AWS) using Amazon's managed Elasticsearch Service (Amazon ES). It covers the key features of Amazon ES, a step-by-step guide to creating a new domain, and methods for ingesting and searching data. Additionally, it addresses how Amazon ES manages various administrative tasks and its compatibility with open-source Elasticsearch APIs.

feed.title: The Business Ethics Leadership Alliance Welcomes New Members, Amazon, Regeneron Pharmaceuticals, and More While Recognizing Companies and Leaders With Exceptional Impact
feed.summary: The Business Ethics Leadership Alliance (BELA) announced new member companies, including Amazon and Regeneron Pharmaceuticals, significantly expanding its community to over 325 organizations. Ethisphere, BELA's founder, also recognized seven companies and individual leaders with BELA Impact Awards for their exceptional commitment to community engagement, growth, and transformation in ethical business practices. The awards highlight crucial contributions to the compliance and ethics profession amidst ongoing global challenges.

feed.title: An FAQ on the Matter protocol
feed.summary: This article provides an FAQ on the Matter protocol, which is a new smart home interoperability standard launched by major tech companies. It explains what Matter and Thread are, how they will affect existing smart home technologies like Zigbee and Z-Wave, and what the implications are for consumers, hardware manufacturers, and professional installers. The article also touches on the potential impact on open-source platforms like Home Assistant.

feed.title: AT&T signing $43B deal to combine media business with Discovery
feed.summary: AT&T announced a $43 billion deal to spin off its WarnerMedia content unit and merge it with Discovery, creating a new media giant. This move aims to better compete with streaming services like Netflix and Disney, with current AT&T shareholders retaining 71 percent of the new company. Discovery CEO David Zaslav will lead the combined entity, marking a significant strategic shift for AT&T.

feed.title: HPE bets on Europe’s Gaia-X cloud and data services initiative
feed.summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) is launching its HPE Solution Framework for Gaia-X, aligning with Europe's initiative to create a unified, data-privacy-protected cloud and data services ecosystem. Gaia-X aims to reduce Europe's reliance on non-European cloud providers by fostering an interoperable data exchange governed by European laws. HPE's framework, built on the HPE Ezmeral Software Platform, will help companies participate in this decentralized environment, emphasizing secure data operations and allowing businesses to maintain ownership and control over their data.

feed.title: Wholesalers are testing out digital marketplaces
feed.summary: The wholesale sector is increasingly adopting digital marketplace models, with large players like United Natural Foods Inc. launching their own platforms to attract hyperlocal vendors and serve small-to-medium businesses. This shift is driven by the pandemic's impact on traditional trade shows and the development of sophisticated software that can handle the complexities of B2B transactions. The trend suggests a significant movement towards online B2B marketplaces, mirroring the growth seen in consumer-oriented e-commerce.

feed.title: Walmart sales boosted by US stimulus cheques
feed.summary: Walmart reported better-than-expected first-quarter results, driven by US government stimulus payments that encouraged shoppers to spend on clothing and home goods. The company raised its annual earnings forecast and observed a 6 percent increase in comparable sales and a 37 percent rise in online sales. CEO Doug McMillon noted a strong desire among customers to return to shopping, contributing to increased transactions and sales in categories like travel items.

feed.title: Woodside Energy adds actual robots into code pipeline stages
feed.summary: Woodside Energy has detailed its continuous integration and continuous deployment (CI/CD) pipeline for robot code, which involves rigorous testing in labs and staging areas before deployment to remote production sites like its Pluto LNG facility. This methodical approach ensures the robots perform as expected in the field, with the long-term goal of enabling remote operations without human supervision. The company aims for these robots to eventually interact with their environment beyond just monitoring.

feed.title: Where and how to buy Alphabet (GOOGL) stock
feed.summary: This guide provides a comprehensive overview for newcomers interested in buying Alphabet (GOOGL) stock. It covers essential steps such as choosing a trading platform, funding an account, deciding investment amounts, and differentiating between buying shares or ETFs. The article also explains various order types and the importance of monitoring GOOGL's performance.

feed.title: JBG SMITH Secures Approval for National Landing Apartments
feed.summary: JBG SMITH has received final approvals for two residential projects at 2000 and 2001 S. Bell St. in Arlington, Va.'s National Landing area. These projects will comprise 758 units and 22,000 square feet of retail space across two towers, with construction potentially starting in early 2022. The development is part of a community benefits agreement with Arlington County, including dedicating affordable housing units and contributing to an affordable housing fund.

feed.title: Amazon sets opening date for its first grocery store in D.C. area. More locations poised to follow.
feed.summary: Amazon has announced the opening date for its first grocery store in the D.C. area, located in Franconia, Virginia, at the Festival at Manchester Lakes shopping center. The store is scheduled to open on May 24 and marks the e-commerce giant's first physical grocery presence in the region, with more locations expected to follow. This development was initially reported last August under the codename "Mendel’s Market."

feed.title: Target Sales Hit $23.8B in Q1
feed.summary: Target reported a significant increase in its Q1 2021 sales, reaching $23.8 billion, a 23% rise from the previous year. This growth was largely attributed to an enthusiastic return to in-store shopping by consumers, with same-store sales up 18%. The company's "stores-as-hubs" model continued to be effective, fulfilling three-quarters of digital orders and over 95% of same-day services.

feed.title: Amazon.com faces five new racial, gender bias lawsuits
feed.summary: Amazon.com Inc. is facing five new lawsuits filed by women in corporate and warehouse management roles, alleging gender bias, racial bias, or both. The lawsuits accuse Amazon of favoring men, allowing workplace denigration, and retaliating against complainants. Amazon denies the accusations and states it does not tolerate discrimination.

feed.title: JPMorgan launching business arm to address cost, quality of employer healthcare
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase is launching a new business arm called Morgan Health to address the cost and quality of employer-sponsored healthcare, building on lessons learned from its dissolved Haven venture with Amazon and Berkshire Hathaway. JPMorgan plans to invest $250 million in healthcare solutions, initially focusing on its own employees before aiming to serve as a model for other employers. Morgan Health will partner with healthcare organizations, including CVS Health, to enhance care options and boost health equity.

feed.title: JPMorgan Chase launches new health-care business after winding down Amazon-Berkshire venture
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase announced the launch of Morgan Health, a new health-care business aimed at improving medical care quality for its 165,000 U.S. employees and their families. This initiative comes after the disbandment of Haven, a joint venture with Amazon and Berkshire Hathaway. Morgan Health will be led by Dan Mendelson and will have $250 million for venture investments in promising health-care solutions, focusing on direct partnerships with providers and preventative care.

feed.title: JPMorgan Chase Launches In-House Healthcare Company, Morgan Health
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase has launched its own healthcare company, Morgan Health, after the dissolution of its joint venture with Amazon and Berkshire Hathaway. The new unit will focus on improving medical care for the banking giant's 165,000 employees and their families, with an initial investment of $250 million earmarked for promising healthcare solutions. JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon stated the goal is to address long-standing healthcare issues and achieve significant improvements.

feed.title: Sprouts accelerates efforts to stand out with unique products
feed.summary: Sprouts Farmers Market is intensifying its focus on selling unique, wellness-oriented products not found at other retailers, aiming to differentiate itself and attract "health enthusiasts." CEO Jack Sinclair stated that the company is positioning itself as a specialty food business rather than a conventional grocer, which allows it to thrive in its e-commerce operations with higher-margin, attribute-based products. This strategy has led to a significant portion of its inventory being unique compared to competitors, and Sprouts sees opportunities as other retailers like Whole Foods diversify into more conventional offerings.

feed.title: Oracle, office deal flow, remote work all key topics of Williamson Inc. economic development panel
feed.summary: A panel of economic developers organized by Williamson Inc. discussed key trends affecting the area, including rising office deal flow, the competition between Oracle and Amazon, and the impact of remote work. A key takeaway was the challenge and opportunity for states to support companies with remote workers. The discussion also touched upon recent job relocations and local business surveys.

feed.title: Schlumberger Announces Collaboration with AWS to Deploy DELFI Petrotechnical Suite on AWS
feed.summary: Schlumberger announced a collaboration with Amazon Web Services (AWS) to deploy its DELFI* cognitive E&P environment and core petrotechnical applications on the cloud. This partnership will give AWS customers access to Schlumberger's AI-enhanced applications and AWS's high-performance computing infrastructure, enabling faster and better decision-making from subsurface data. The goal is to expand access to the DELFI environment, allowing more customers to leverage advanced digital solutions for improved productivity and performance in the energy sector.

feed.title: JD Logistics IPO plans fail to lift JD.com shares
feed.summary: Despite its logistics arm, JD Logistics, preparing for a substantial IPO that could raise up to $3.4 billion, JD.com's share price has been on a downward trend since February. The company's stock has underperformed the S&P 500 over the past 12 months and has seen further decline since the IPO's approval. The JD Logistics IPO is viewed as a critical test for investor interest in Chinese tech stocks, a sector that has faced regulatory scrutiny and declining performance in recent months.

feed.title: Amazon shutting down Prime Now, moving two-hour delivery to Amazon app
feed.summary: Amazon is discontinuing its standalone Prime Now app, integrating its two-hour delivery services for essential goods and groceries directly into the main Amazon app and website. This move aims to streamline the shopping experience for customers by offering all of Amazon's services from a single platform. The integration will allow users to shop from Whole Foods and other local stores with expedited delivery.

feed.title: JPMorgan Chase unveils solo healthcare venture
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase has launched Morgan Health, a new healthcare venture with $250 million to invest in innovations aimed at improving medical care quality, health equity, and cost control for its 165,000 employees and their families. This initiative comes four months after the dissolution of Haven, a joint healthcare venture involving Amazon, Berkshire Hathaway, and JPMorgan. Led by Dan Mendelson, Morgan Health will focus on digital tools, analytics, preventative care for conditions like maternal health, cardiovascular disease, and diabetes, and will collaborate with other organizations rather than building new technologies from scratch.

feed.title: P&G Earns Seventh Straight Gartner Supply Chain Mastery Award
feed.summary: P&G has received its seventh consecutive Gartner Supply Chain Mastery Award, an achievement that highlights its long-term excellence in supply chain management. This recognition comes despite the extreme disruptions caused by the pandemic, which tested P&G's supply chain significantly. P&G is one of only five companies, including Apple, Amazon, McDonald’s, and Unilever, to be recognized in the elite "Master" category, which acknowledges consistent top performance over many years.

feed.title: The Curious Case of a Toys ‘R’ Us Killer
feed.summary: This article traces the unexpected connection between Vornado Realty, a prominent real estate firm, and the demise of Toys 'R' Us, highlighting Vornado's own twisting corporate history. It reveals how Vornado, originally an appliance manufacturer and then a discount retailer (Two Guys), transformed into a real estate giant by leveraging its suburban land holdings. The piece argues that Vornado's trajectory and engagement with Toys 'R' Us exemplify broader, long-term trends of offshoring and financialization within the American economy.

feed.title: Amazon nears deal to buy MGM Studios
feed.summary: Amazon is reportedly close to acquiring MGM Holdings for nearly $9 billion, marking its second-largest acquisition. The deal would significantly bolster Amazon's streaming service, Prime Video, with MGM's extensive library of classic films and TV shows including the James Bond franchise. This move reflects the increasing consolidation in the media industry driven by the streaming wars.

feed.title: Huge Fort Worth shipping hub changes hands
feed.summary: A large Fort Worth industrial shipping hub, leased to Amazon, has been sold to a real estate investment trust. The Interstate Crossing distribution center, over 1 million square feet, was developed by Hunt Southwest and acquired by a fund organized by San Diego-based Realty Income Corp. This sale reflects the ongoing strong investor interest in warehouse and distribution properties, especially significant in one of the country's largest warehouse building markets.

feed.title: Amazon deal to acquire MGM movie studio could come Tuesday
feed.summary: Amazon is reportedly close to finalizing a deal to acquire Metro-Goldwyn-Mayer (MGM) movie studio as early as Tuesday for nearly $9 billion. This acquisition would significantly bolster Amazon's Prime Video streaming service by adding MGM's extensive library, which includes popular franchises like James Bond, Pink Panther, RoboCop, and Rocky, as well as films like "The Silence of the Lambs." The move represents Amazon's largest push into Hollywood and its biggest acquisition since Whole Foods in 2017.

feed.title: Twilio CEO Jeff Lawson reveals how founding StubHub and a skate shop led him to Twilio
feed.summary: Twilio CEO Jeff Lawson shares his entrepreneurial journey, beginning with a childhood fascination with computers and early ventures like the app Notes4Free and co-founding StubHub. He discusses how his diverse experiences, including even opening a skate shop, eventually led him to found Twilio, driven by a passion for software's ability to iterate and serve customers. Lawson emphasizes key lessons learned such as ruthlessly removing friction for customer attraction, the importance of listening to customers even regarding sales teams, and the responsibility of giving back to society.

feed.title: Oracle debuts its first Arm-based cloud instances
feed.summary: Oracle has launched its first Arm-based cloud instances, OCI Ampere A1 Compute, offering customers more flexibility and high performance at lower costs for general-purpose and cloud-native workloads. This move signifies Oracle's increased investment in the Arm ecosystem, providing a compelling alternative to x86-based instances and competing with rival cloud providers by offering attractive pricing and scaling options. The company is also expanding developer resources and tools to encourage Arm-based application development on its cloud platform, including free tier offerings and a new development stack.

feed.title: Oracle launches Arm-based cloud computing service using Ampere chips
feed.summary: Oracle Corp has launched a new cloud computing service that utilizes Arm-based data center chips from Ampere Computing, becoming the second major cloud provider after Amazon Web Services to offer such a service. This move aims to provide customers with more value and choice, challenging the long-standing dominance of Intel and AMD in the cloud chip market. Oracle is also promoting initiatives to expand software compatibility with Arm chips.

feed.title: Microsoft teams up with Accenture and Goldman Sachs to create energy efficient software
feed.summary: Microsoft, Accenture, and Goldman Sachs, along with other organizations, are forming the Green Software Foundation to develop and share methods for creating software that produces fewer carbon emissions in data centers. Data centers currently account for about 1% of global electricity demand, projected to rise significantly, prompting the need for "green software engineering" skills. The foundation aims to build tools, establish standards for measuring software's climate impact, and provide training for engineers.

feed.title: App Store Arguments
feed.summary: This article analyzes the legal, pragmatic, duopoly, moral, and anti-steering arguments surrounding Apple's App Store, particularly in light of the Epic Games v. Apple trial. The author suggests that while Apple has a strong legal standing based on Supreme Court precedents, there are valid counterarguments regarding competition, developer relationships, and the overall health of the iOS ecosystem. The piece concludes by suggesting Apple should consider conceding some ground to developers to foster a more open and innovative environment, especially given global regulatory scrutiny.

feed.title: JPMorgan Chase Launches New Unit to Improve Care Quality for Employees
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase is investing up to $250 million in a new venture called Morgan Health, aiming to improve health outcomes and promote health equity for its employees. This initiative follows the dissolution of its previous Haven collaborative with Amazon and Berkshire Hathaway. Morgan Health plans to collaborate with leading healthcare organizations to develop new models for care improvement, focusing on behavioral health, disease management, and addressing healthcare disparities.

feed.title: Globe, PLDT say content now important part of business
feed.summary: Globe Telecom and PLDT view content creation and bundling as a critical component for future growth and customer retention. Globe expects its content business and "customer-first" initiatives to drive growth, expanding on existing partnerships and producing original content through Globe Studios. PLDT's Chairman, Manuel V. Pangilinan, emphasizes the need for synergy among media companies within the group to provide converged content, particularly video, and enhance the overall customer experience for wireless users.

feed.title: Amazon agrees to buy MGM film studio for $8.45 billion
authors: By Asa Hiken; By Adrianne Pasquarelli; By E.J. Schultz; By Brian Bonilla; By Parker Herren
feed.summary: Amazon has reached an agreement to acquire MGM film studio for $8.45 billion. This acquisition will significantly expand Amazon's content library, bolstering its presence in the entertainment industry and providing a vast catalog for its Prime Video service. The deal highlights the increasing consolidation in the media landscape.

feed.title: Amazon buys MGM as D.C. suit charges its monopolistic practices violate antitrust laws
feed.summary: Amazon announced its acquisition of MGM for $8.5 billion, capitalizing on the film industry's struggles. This comes as Washington D.C. Attorney General Karl Racine filed an antitrust suit against Amazon, alleging that its contractual terms force third-party sellers to offer products at lower prices on Amazon, thereby harming both sellers and customers through inflated fees and prices. The lawsuit highlights Amazon's significant control over online retail and questions the monopolistic practices of platform companies.

feed.title: Hedge Fund and Insider Trading News: Carl Icahn, Warren Buffett, Bill Ackman, Ray Dalio, Elliott Management, Assurant Inc. (AIZ), Innoviva Inc (INVA), and More
feed.summary: This article covers recent news in the hedge fund and insider trading world, featuring prominent investors like Carl Icahn, Bill Ackman, and Elliott Management. Key topics include Carl Icahn's activism, Bill Ackman's SPAC deal and market outlook, Elliott Management's strategy regarding GlaxoSmithKline, and Amazon's acquisition of MGM bolstering hedge fund profits. The article also touches on increasing data demands driving hedge fund operational costs and hedge fund assets reaching a new high of over $4 trillion.

feed.title: Amazon's major acquisitions over the years
feed.summary: Amazon has acquired MGM for $8.45 billion, marking its second-largest acquisition and deepening its push into entertainment. This deal grants Amazon a vast library of films and TV shows for its Prime video streaming service. The article outlines Amazon's significant acquisitions over the past two decades, including Whole Foods Market, Twitch, Ring, and PillPack.

feed.title: Amazon shareholder meeting: Push to put an hourly worker on board
feed.summary: Labour activists are pushing for an hourly worker to join Amazon's board of directors at its shareholder meeting, following a failed union drive. The proposal, backed by Oxfam America and recommended by proxy adviser ISS, aims to elevate employee concerns amidst allegations of monopolistic practices and environmental racism. However, Amazon's board opposes the resolution, and some proxy advisers are concerned about board independence.

feed.title: Apple Faces Criticism Over Its Cooperation With China
feed.summary: Apple is facing increasing criticism for its cooperation with Chinese laws, which involve sharing consumer data and censoring content, amid a wider technological and economic competition between the U.S. and China. The article also discusses the US Senate's "Endless Frontier Act" aimed at countering China's tech advancements, China's efforts to influence Latin American and African telecom markets, and its crackdown on cryptocurrency and its own tech giants.

feed.title: MGM to Be Acquired by Amazon for $8.45 Billion
feed.summary: Amazon announced its acquisition of Beverly Hills-based Metro-Goldwyn-Mayer Studios Inc. for $8.45 billion. This deal gives Amazon access to MGM's extensive library of 4,000 films and 17,000 television shows, aiming to enhance Prime Video's competitive edge against other streaming services. The acquisition, subject to regulatory approval, will allow Amazon to "reimagine and develop" MGM's intellectual property for the 21st century.

feed.title: American Express Sets Up Autonomous Checkout At Barclays Center
feed.summary: American Express has launched an autonomous checkout store at Brooklyn's Barclays Center, home of the New York Nets, to allow cardholders to quickly purchase concessions and merchandise. This initiative, similar to Amazon Go stores, aims to enhance customer experience by reducing wait times and addressing contactless payment preferences heightened by COVID-19. The store tracks items using weight-sensitive shelf sensors, with entry exclusive to Amex cardholders.

feed.title: FOS PM: Dick's Crushes Earnings
feed.summary: Dick's Sporting Goods reported record first-quarter earnings, with revenue soaring 119% to $2.92 billion and net income reaching $361.8 million. This surge was attributed to increased interest in golf and a resurgence in team sports, alongside strong performance in outdoor activities and athleisure. Additionally, Amazon acquired MGM Studios for $8.45 billion to expand its media library, and billionaire Joseph Tsai added a Las Vegas lacrosse expansion team to his sports portfolio.

feed.title: During Investor Day, Kontoor Details the Benefits of Casting a Wide Net
feed.summary: Kontoor Brands held its first-ever virtual investor day, outlining strategies for significant revenue and consumer growth for its Lee and Wrangler brands. Despite initial COVID-19 impacts, both brands are bouncing back, with plans for geographic expansion, category extensions, and digital initiatives. The company anticipates substantial e-commerce revenue increases and aims to boost Wrangler's revenue to $1.7 billion and Lee's to $1 billion by 2023 through product elevation, new categories like outdoor wear, and strategic collaborations.

feed.title: Amazon brings James Bond, Rocky to fight Netflix with $8.5 bln MGM buy
feed.summary: Amazon is acquiring MGM, the studio behind James Bond and Rocky, for $8.45 billion to expand its content library and compete more effectively with streaming rivals like Netflix and Disney+. This acquisition will bolster Amazon's Prime Video service by adding a vast catalog of films and TV shows, including valuable intellectual property that Amazon plans to reimagine for the 21st century. The deal, Amazon's second-largest after Whole Foods, highlights the ongoing consolidation in the media industry as companies vie for compelling content to attract and retain streaming subscribers.

feed.title: Sony Pictures Not for Sale, Says CEO Yoshida Kenichiro
feed.summary: Sony Group CEO Yoshida Kenichiro has stated that Sony Pictures is not for sale and will not be spun off, despite industry speculation fueled by recent mergers and acquisitions like Amazon's purchase of MGM. Sony's strategy involves operating as an independent studio, supplying content to streamers, and leveraging its various entertainment divisions, such as gaming and anime, to create content. The company has secured licensing deals with Netflix and Disney+ and plans to produce more films for direct release on streaming platforms while retaining ownership.

feed.title: Bitcoin and Ethereum are now, decentralized apps are the future
feed.summary: Olaf Carlson-Wee, founder of Polychain Capital, believes decentralized web applications (DAOs) will be the next major trend in crypto, following the success of Bitcoin, Ethereum, and DeFi. He envisions decentralized versions of popular social media and chat apps owned by communities via crypto tokens, starting with simpler applications like photo hosting or messaging apps before moving to more complex platforms. Carlson-Wee also notes the rapid growth of decentralized exchanges (DEXes) like Uniswap, highlighting their collision course with regulators due to potential money laundering concerns.

feed.title: S&P 500 trading guide: constituents, market hours and h
feed.summary: This guide provides an in-depth look at the S&P 500, a key benchmark for U.S. stock market performance. It covers the index's constituents, listing criteria, calculation methods, factors affecting its price, and average annual returns. The article also details various ways to trade the S&P 500, including CFDs, futures, options, and ETFs, and discusses strategies for shorting the index.

feed.title: In Current M&A Moment, Lionsgate A Prime Target As CEO Jon Feltheimer Downplays “Concept Of Scale”
feed.summary: Lionsgate CEO Jon Feltheimer acknowledged the recent media merger deals as a validation of content value but emphasized the company's focus on its own strategy, downplaying the "concept of scale." Despite this, Lionsgate is widely considered a prime takeover target due to its valuable library, Starz subscribers, and manageable size, especially following major deals like Amazon's acquisition of MGM. Analysts suggest it could be worth at least $8 billion.

feed.title: How Vimeo went from CollegeHumor side project to a multibillion-dollar company
feed.summary: Vimeo, once a side project of CollegeHumor, has grown into a multibillion-dollar public software company. Initially acquired by IAC almost by accident, Vimeo pivoted from being a YouTube competitor to building video software for businesses under the leadership of Anjali Sud. The company recently went public with over 200 million users and 1.6 million paying subscribers, generating substantial revenue.

feed.title: Hundreds of Companies Unite to Oppose Voting Limits, but Others Abstain (Published 2021)
feed.summary: Hundreds of major companies, including Amazon, Google, G.M., and Starbucks, signed a statement opposing "any discriminatory legislation" that makes it harder to vote. This initiative, organized by former American Express CEO Kenneth Chenault and Merck CEO Kenneth Frazier, marks the largest show of corporate solidarity against Republican-led efforts to enact new election rules nationwide. The move comes despite calls from senior Republicans for companies to avoid political issues.

feed.title: Higg launches customer-facing transparency program
feed.summary: The Sustainable Apparel Coalition (SAC) and its partner Higg have launched the first phase of a transparency program to share data on the environmental impact of apparel and footwear products, starting with materials. The program introduces the Higg Index Sustainability Profile and Materials seal, utilizing independently reviewed data from the Higg Materials Sustainability Index (MSI). Brands like Norrøna and Salomon are early adopters, with plans to expand the initiative to include manufacturing, corporate responsibility, and social data by early 2023.

feed.title: Docker Honeypot Reveals Cryptojacking as Most Common Cloud Threat
feed.summary: Palo Alto Networks' Unit 42 deployed a honeypot mimicking a misconfigured Docker daemon to observe cloud-related threats between March and April 2021. The research found that over 75% of the 850 attacks recorded were cryptojacking, with Kinsing being the most prevalent malware. Attackers exploit misconfigured Docker daemons to gain full control, deploy malicious containers, and often escalate privileges to the host system.

feed.title: Amazon Fresh opens first D.C.-area location. In this full-size grocery store, no cashier is needed.
feed.summary: Amazon has opened its first Amazon Fresh grocery store in the D.C. area, located at the Manchester Lakes shopping center in Franconia. The store offers a cashier-less shopping experience, where Prime customers can use Amazon Dash carts. Shoppers can also use Alexa for in-store assistance.

feed.title: AR’s role in shopping poised to swell in next 5 years, Publicis and Snap find
feed.summary: A new study by Publicis Media, Snap, and Alter Agents indicates that augmented reality (AR) significantly influences consumer shopping habits, especially among Snapchat users who are 56% more likely to use branded AR lenses and increase their likelihood to purchase. The pandemic accelerated AR adoption in shopping, and three out of four respondents believe AR will play a larger role in retail over the next five years. Snap continues to expand its commerce capabilities, integrating AR into new features and partnerships to meet evolving consumer expectations and leverage AR's increasing utility in the shopping journey.

feed.title: Coinbase crypto partner Circle raises $440 million from Fidelity and others
feed.summary: Cryptocurrency startup Circle, a Coinbase partner, has successfully raised $440 million in new funding from investors including Fidelity and FTX. This funding round highlights the growing investor appetite for crypto companies, coinciding with Coinbase's market debut and Circle's own plans to go public via a SPAC at a $4 billion valuation. Circle is increasingly focusing on its USD Coin (USDC) stablecoin, which has seen significant growth and adoption for digital payments.

feed.title: Mountain View self-driving trucking company Aurora Innovation expanding in Dallas area
feed.summary: Aurora Innovation, a self-driving trucking company backed by Amazon and Hyundai, is expanding its operations in the Dallas area to accommodate its growing workforce. This expansion comes less than a year after the company announced its intention to test its self-driving technology in Texas. Aurora Innovation's CEO, Chris Urmson, previously led Google's self-driving car unit.

feed.title: How Nvidia plans to own the data center with AI
feed.summary: Nvidia aims to revolutionize enterprise computing by making data centers run 10 times faster at one-tenth the cost, leveraging its strong position in AI architectures. The company plans to disrupt Intel's x86 dominance through Arm-based architectures, integrated chip designs, and a compelling software consumption model. The article highlights Nvidia's strategic moves, financial standing, and the potential impact of its proposed acquisition of Arm Ltd. on the data center market.

feed.title: Everything to know about the Huntington Shopping Center renovation plans
feed.summary: The Huntington Shopping Center is set for a $40 million renovation, which will see much of the existing structure demolished to make way for a new building anchored by a Whole Foods grocery store. The plans include significant landscaping updates, new facades, wider sidewalks, and two potential standalone buildings for restaurants or high-end retail, despite an overall 22% reduction in retail space. While the architectural renderings have been approved, the site plan is still awaiting final approval, and the future of existing tenants remains uncertain, though Nordstrom Rack is confirmed to close.

feed.title: Fifth Third Bank warns of text scams about online banking
feed.summary: Fifth Third Bank is warning customers about text message scams claiming a "temporary hold" has been placed on their online banking accounts. These texts are fraudulent, and the bank advises caution against clicking links or sharing personal information. Customers should be suspicious of urgent requests for account numbers or Social Security numbers and can call Fifth Third directly if unsure about a message's legitimacy.

feed.title: Grab, GoTo, and Sea: Who Has the Formula For Superapp Supremacy in Southeast Asia?
feed.summary: This article analyzes the three-way battle for superapp supremacy in Southeast Asia between Grab, GoTo, and Sea Limited. It profiles each company, highlighting their strengths and weaknesses, and scores them across five strategic areas: ridesharing and food delivery, entertainment, ecommerce, payment apps, and founder/CEO leadership. The author concludes that while the race appears even, each company has distinct advantages, with Grab strong in payments and regional expansion, Sea in entertainment and e-commerce, and GoTo's strength lying in Indonesia.

feed.title: KKR, CD&R to take data analytics firm Cloudera private for $4.7 billion
feed.summary: Private-equity firms KKR and Clayton Dubilier & Rice (CD&R) are set to acquire cloud-based data analytics company Cloudera for $4.7 billion, taking it private. Cloudera's shares jumped approximately 25% in premarket trading on news of the $16 per share all-cash offer. The deal includes a 30-day "go-shop" period allowing Cloudera to consider alternative offers and is expected to close in the second half of the year.

feed.title: KKR, CD&R Strike $5.3 Billion Deal to Buy Cloudera
feed.summary: Private-equity firms KKR & Co. and Clayton Dubilier & Rice LLC have agreed to acquire software company Cloudera Inc. for approximately $5.3 billion, taking the company private. The deal values Cloudera at $16 per share, representing a 24% premium over its closing price on the preceding Friday. Cloudera, founded by engineers from major tech companies, struggled to adapt to the public cloud market, facing stiff competition from larger firms.

feed.title: KKR, CD&R To Acquire Cloudera At $5.3B Valuation
feed.summary: KKR and Clayton Dubilier & Rice LLC have agreed to acquire Cloudera Inc. in an all-cash transaction valued at $5.3 billion, effectively taking the company private. The acquisition price of $16 per share represents a 24% premium over Cloudera's closing price on May 28, 2021. Cloudera, an early player in open-source software, has faced challenges in adapting to the public cloud environment and competition from major players like Amazon Web Services.

feed.title: KKR, CD&R take data analytics firm Cloudera private for $4.7 bln
feed.summary: Private equity firms Clayton Dubilier & Rice LLC (CD&R) and KKR & Co (KKR.N) are taking cloud-based data analytics company Cloudera Inc (CLDR.N) private for $4.7 billion. Cloudera's shares jumped 23.9% following the announcement, nearing the offer price of $16 per share. The move is intended to help Cloudera transition more smoothly into hybrid cloud solutions without the pressure of quarterly reporting.

feed.title: Etsy is acquiring UK-based social selling site Depop for $1.625B in a mostly cash deal
feed.summary: Etsy is acquiring UK-based social selling site Depop for $1.625 billion in a mostly cash deal, marking Etsy's largest acquisition to date. This move is significant for e-commerce, especially in targeting younger consumers, as 90% of Depop's 21 million users are under 26. The acquisition aims to broaden Etsy's reach to millennial and Gen Z shoppers and strengthen its presence in the U.S. and U.K. markets.

feed.title: Joby’s Plan for Air Taxis Takes Shape
feed.summary: Joby Aviation, a startup developing electric vertical takeoff and landing (eVTOL) aircraft, plans a commercial launch in 2024 and is going public via a SPAC. The company recently demonstrated its five-seat electric aircraft, which is designed to carry passengers quietly and efficiently. Joby is among many companies, including aerospace giants and other startups, competing to develop and launch air taxi services.

feed.title: Etsy Buys Depop, Fashion Resale Site Popular With Gen Z, for $1.6 Billion
authors: Charity L. Scott and Colin Kellaher
feed.summary: Etsy Inc. announced its agreement to acquire Depop, a privately held fashion marketplace, for $1.63 billion. This acquisition is a significant move into the burgeoning secondhand goods market, giving Etsy access to Depop's younger, fashion-conscious demographic. Depop experienced substantial growth last year, with its gross merchandise sales and revenue more than doubling to approximately $650 million and $70 million, respectively.

feed.title: Pitney Bowes introduces ParcelPoint Smart Lockers
feed.summary: Pitney Bowes has launched ParcelPoint Smart Lockers, an expansion of its parcel locker systems, to address the surging demand for e-commerce and secure package management. These lockers, managed by Pitney Bowes’ SaaS technology, offer remote management and aim to streamline parcel and mail delivery in various settings. The global smart parcel locker market is projected to reach over $1.4 billion by 2027, driven by the exponential growth in parcel volume.

feed.title: Serious Injury Rate for Amazon Workers Is Nearly Double the Industry Average
feed.summary: New data from OSHA and the Strategic Organizing Center (SOC) reveals that Amazon warehouse workers sustain serious injuries at nearly double the industry average. In 2020, Amazon recorded 5.9 serious injury incidents per 100 full-time workers, compared to 3.1 at other warehouses, with workers requiring longer recovery times. The high injury rates are largely attributed to Amazon's strict production speed requirements, with automation further exacerbating the issue.

feed.title: ‘The Guncle’ Movie in the Works From Lionsgate
feed.summary: Lionsgate has acquired the film rights to Steven Rowley's novel "The Guncle" for a movie adaptation. Rowley will adapt and executive produce the film, which tells the story of a reclusive gay former TV star who takes in his niece and nephew after their mother's death. Kristin Burr and Rob Weisbach will produce the project.

feed.title: Microsoft’s Irish subsidiary posted £220bn profit in single year | Tax havens
feed.summary: Microsoft's Irish subsidiary, Microsoft Round Island One, recorded a profit of $315 billion (£222 billion) in a single year, which is nearly three-quarters of Ireland's GDP, despite having no employees other than directors. The company is resident for tax purposes in Bermuda, which has no corporation tax, allowing it to pay no tax on income. This news emerged as G7 finance ministers discussed global minimum corporate tax rates to combat multinational tax avoidance.

feed.title: Walgreens closes $6.3B sale of Alliance Healthcare to AmerisourceBergen
feed.summary: AmerisourceBergen has finalized its near $6.3 billion acquisition of Walgreens' Alliance Healthcare, a major European drug wholesaler. Walgreens plans to use the proceeds to reduce its debt by $3.3 billion and accelerate its growth strategy for its core retail pharmacy and healthcare businesses. The acquisition expands AmerisourceBergen's global distribution network and manufacturer services, positioning both companies for future growth and innovation in the healthcare sector.

feed.title: With Depop, Etsy sets sights on Gen Z, secondhand
feed.summary: Etsy has acquired the secondhand fashion app Depop for $1.6 billion, a strategic move to penetrate the Gen Z market and expand its presence in the burgeoning resale industry. The acquisition allows Etsy to tap into Depop's young user base (nearly 90% are under 26) and its trendy vintage and DIY fashion offerings, diversifying from Etsy's traditional handmade goods focus. While some Depop users express concerns about potential fee increases, the deal positions both companies for continued growth in the competitive online marketplace and resale sectors.

feed.title: DLocal raises $75M in IPO, spearheads emerging markets
feed.summary: DLocal, a cross-border payments company, successfully raised $75 million in its initial public offering, with most shares sold by existing stockholders. The company's stock jumped 54% on its first day, increasing its valuation to $9 billion. DLocal specializes in providing local payment methods and regulatory compliance for global merchants in emerging markets, a sector that saw significant growth during the pandemic.

feed.title: Media and data firm Thomson Reuters faces pressure to ditch Ice contracts
feed.summary: Thomson Reuters is facing increased pressure from shareholders and advocacy groups to terminate its contracts with US Immigration and Customs Enforcement (Ice) due to human rights concerns. Critics argue that the company's data-providing software, Clear, facilitates the tracking and deportation of immigrants. A shareholder resolution calling for an assessment of human rights impacts related to its Ice contracts will be voted on, despite opposition from the company's board of directors.

feed.title: In a Leaked Pamphlet, Amazon Refers to Its Warehouse Workers as "Industrial Athletes"
feed.summary: A leaked Amazon pamphlet, distributed to warehouse workers, refers to them as "industrial athletes" who need to train for demanding physical tasks like walking "up to 13 miles a day" or lifting "a total of 20,000 pounds" per shift. This revelation comes as Amazon faces scrutiny for its high rate of work-related injuries, particularly musculoskeletal disorders. Critics argue that focusing on "athletic" training distracts from the core issues of rapid work pace, physical exertion, and long shifts that contribute to worker injuries.

feed.title: Amazon exec named Beyond Meat CFO
feed.summary: Beyond Meat has appointed Phil Hardin, a former Amazon vice president of finance, as its new CFO, effective next month. Hardin will replace Mark Nelson, who led the company through its 2019 IPO. Additionally, Beyond Meat announced Deanna Jurgens as chief growth officer and Jackie Trask as chief people officer, aiming to leverage their expertise for strategic growth and brand disruption.

feed.title: dLocal raises $617.65M in IPO
feed.summary: dLocal, a cross-border payments platform, successfully raised $617.65M in its US initial public offering, pricing 29.4 million shares at $21 each, valuing the company at $6.06B. This IPO comes as digital payments and e-commerce are rapidly growing, with dLocal reporting an 88.4% revenue increase in 2020 to $104M. The company serves over 330 global enterprise clients, including major brands like Spotify, Zara, and Amazon, providing a localized platform for various payment methods.

feed.title: Staples returns to Office Depot with a $1 billion offer for its consumer business. (Published 2021)
feed.summary: Staples has made a $1 billion all-cash offer for Office Depot's consumer business, which includes the Office Depot and OfficeMax retail chains. This new offer comes after previous attempts by Staples to acquire all of Office Depot were rebuffed due to antitrust concerns and a prior blocked merger attempt. The retail landscape has changed significantly since then, with the rise of e-commerce.

feed.title: 3 Bottomed Out Growth Stocks with Huge Upside — TIGR, FUBO, SKLZ
feed.summary: This article identifies three growth stocks—TIGR (UP Fintech Holding Limited), FUBO (FuboTV Inc), and SKLZ (Skillz Inc)—that are showing signs of bottoming out and offer potential for significant upside. The author suggests these stocks present low-risk entry opportunities before they experience further growth. It includes timestamps for discussing each stock in a linked video resource.

feed.title: Kroger to hire 10,000 workers across its grocery brands
feed.summary: Kroger announced a hiring event for Thursday to fill 10,000 jobs across its retail, e-commerce, pharmacy, manufacturing, and logistical operations. The company plans to raise its average associate wage to $16 per hour and offers various benefits. This nationwide hybrid hiring event comes as retailers face a competitive labor market, and Kroger seeks to employ staff similar to its pre-pandemic levels after an initial surge in hiring.

feed.title: How Apple Lossless Audio Challenges Streaming Rivals
feed.summary: Apple is entering the high-fidelity audio market with lossless, high-resolution, and spatial audio for Apple Music, aiming to differentiate itself from competitors like Spotify and Amazon Prime. This move challenges streaming rivals by offering superior sound quality, as lossless audio preserves all original sound data, unlike compressed formats such as MP3s. This strategy focuses on technological advancement to improve the listening experience.

feed.title: Apple is turning privacy into a business advantage, not just a marketing slogan
feed.summary: Apple is increasingly integrating privacy measures into its products and services, differentiating itself from competitors. Recent announcements include features like email tracking prevention, IP address obfuscation, and burner emails, all under the umbrella of iCloud+. The company leverages its self-designed chips and on-device processing capabilities to enhance privacy and introduce new functionalities in areas like health and digital identity, reinforcing user trust and opening new market opportunities.

feed.title: Lord Of The Rings Coming To Build A Bear Workshop
feed.summary: Build-A-Bear Workshop is releasing a new collection featuring characters from J.R.R. Tolkien's The Lord of the Rings, allowing fans to pre-order stuffed bears designed as Frodo, Gandalf, and Gollum. This collection is a limited-edition reissue of previously sold-out collaborations. Fans who purchase the complete set will receive bears pre-loaded with iconic quotes from the films, and Build-A-Bear is encouraging sign-ups for their email list for updates on the release.

feed.title: Investment group buys Kohl's store building in Menomonee Falls
feed.summary: A California investment group, Investment Grade R.E Income Fund LP (IGRE), has purchased the Kohl's department store building in Menomonee Falls for $9.8 million. The 98,500 square-foot building is considered a "flagship store" due to its proximity to Kohl's headquarters and its high performance, including partnerships with Amazon and Sephora. IGRE targets single-tenant, triple-net leased properties with "essential" businesses and investment-grade tenants, making this acquisition ideal for their portfolio.

feed.title: Oracle shows off more ‘Always Free’ cloud resources for developers
feed.summary: Oracle has significantly expanded its "Always Free" cloud services for developers, adding 13 new offerings to its Oracle Cloud Infrastructure platform, bringing the total to 20. These free resources aim to attract developers to OCI by allowing them to experiment with services, including the new Arm-based Ampere A1 Compute, Oracle JSON Database, NoSQL Database Cloud Service, and low-code application development tools. This expansion positions Oracle more competitively against rivals like AWS and Google Cloud by providing substantial free tiers for building and testing end-to-end applications.

feed.title: One of JBG Smith's Amazon point men just left to build National Landing's next big developer
feed.summary: Andy Van Horn, a long-time executive at JBG Smith, has departed the company to join Dweck Properties. He aims to transform Dweck Properties into a major developer, particularly within the National Landing area. This move comes as the region plans for significant development around Amazon's presence.

feed.title: Internet Outage: Top Websites including Amazon, Reddit, Guardian, NYT Go Down Briefly, Service Restored, say Reports
feed.summary: Several top websites, including Amazon, Reddit, The Guardian, and The New York Times, experienced a brief internet outage. The disruption affected major foreign media outlets and the Amazon retail website, with reports suggesting CDN provider services were a factor. Services for most affected sites were restored shortly after the outage.

feed.title: This Color-Changing Spray Keeps Bathrooms Sparkly Clean
feed.summary: The article highlights Kaboom Cleaner as a highly effective solution for bathroom cleaning, boasting over 11,000 5-star ratings on Amazon. It details different Kaboom products, including a color-changing spray for soap scum and a no-drip foam for mold and mildew, emphasizing their ability to tackle tough stains with the power of OxiClean. The author recommends the products for ease of use and affordability, suggesting Amazon as a convenient purchase option.

feed.title: Red Rocks building new 1 million square foot spec building near Amazon, Chewy
feed.summary: Red Rocks Development, the company behind the Amazon distribution facility in Ocala, Florida, has broken ground on a new 1 million square foot speculative industrial building. Located at the Florida Crossroads Logistics Center, this facility will be the largest spec building ever constructed in Ocala. Its strategic location is expected to allow for same-day distribution throughout Florida, making it an ideal hub for logistics.

feed.title: The Secret IRS Files: Trove of Never-Before-Seen Records Reveal How the Wealthiest Avoid Income Tax
feed.summary: ProPublica has obtained a vast cache of IRS data revealing how the wealthiest Americans, including Jeff Bezos, Elon Musk, and Warren Buffett, legally pay minimal federal income taxes despite their immense wealth. The investigation highlights a "true tax rate" much lower than that of ordinary Americans, often achieved by leveraging asset growth, low salaries, and strategic borrowing. The findings challenge the fairness of the U.S. tax system and come amidst renewed calls for tax reforms targeting extreme wealth.

feed.title: Report: Chipotle raises prices to cover increase in worker pay
feed.summary: Chipotle Mexican Grill has raised its menu prices by approximately 4% to help cover the cost of increasing employee wages to an average of $15 per hour. The company announced its intention to raise worker pay in May and had indicated that these costs would be passed on to customers. While ingredient costs are also rising across the industry, Chipotle is not currently planning further price increases to offset those specific costs.

feed.title: New CSPM Functionality Frees Teams from Alert Noise and Blind Spots
feed.summary: Palo Alto Networks has introduced new Cloud Security Posture Management (CSPM) functionality within Prisma Cloud to enhance cloud security by reducing alert noise and blind spots. The update includes features like true internet exposure visibility, visibility-as-code, network data exfiltration detection, anomalous compute provisioning detection, and customizable object-level scanning for AWS S3. These enhancements aim to provide deeper context for incidents, eliminate vague alerts, and improve threat detection for complex cloud architectures.

feed.title: Walmart offers prescription discounts to lure in subscription members
feed.summary: Walmart is introducing a new prescription savings program called Walmart+ Rx for less, offering discounts of up to 85% or even free medications to its Walmart+ subscription members. This initiative aims to incentivize more consumers to join Walmart's subscription service while making healthcare more accessible and affordable. The program will cover a range of health needs, including heart health, mental health, antibiotics, allergies, and diabetes management, and members can access these savings at any Walmart pharmacy with a pharmacy savings card and a valid prescription.

feed.title: Amazon reviewing bids to replace JPMorgan as credit card partner - source
feed.summary: Amazon is reportedly looking for a new credit card partner to replace JPMorgan Chase & Co. The e-commerce giant has issued a 'request for proposal' for its co-branded credit card portfolio, which is valued at over $15 billion in credit card lending. American Express Co and Synchrony Financial are among the companies that have submitted bids for the partnership.

feed.title: Big Data Technology Market to Rise at 14% CAGR till 2027; Growing Internet Penetration will Provide Impetus to Market Growth, says Fortune Business Insights™
feed.summary: The global big data technology market is projected to reach USD 116.07 billion by 2027, growing at a CAGR of 14.0% from USD 41.33 billion in 2019. This growth is driven by increasing internet penetration, adoption of cloud-based platforms, and integration of AI and machine learning. While the COVID-19 pandemic has created mixed fortunes, with increased online data due to remote work, challenges like workforce shortages and budget constraints exist.

feed.title: Luxury E-Commerce Retailer 1stDibs Sets Terms to Go Public
feed.summary: 1stDibs, a luxury e-commerce marketplace specializing in high-end items, is preparing for its initial public offering (IPO) on the Nasdaq Exchange under the ticker symbol "DIBS." The company aims to raise $112 million by offering 5.8 million shares at $18–$21 apiece, which would give it a fully diluted market value of $773 million. While 1stDibs is not yet profitable, its net losses decreased to $12 million in 2020 from $30 million in 2019, with annual revenue reaching $81 million in 2020.

feed.title: BMO Signs Deal With Amazon Web Services to Help Digital Shift
feed.summary: Bank of Montreal (BMO) has selected Amazon Web Services (AWS) as its preferred cloud provider to modernize operations and launch new digital applications.
