This partnership will involve AWS technology for BMO's call centers, remote work tools, and machine-learning capabilities. The financial details of the agreement remain undisclosed.

feed.title: Amazon card portfolio up for grabs with bids from AmEx, Synchrony
feed.summary: American Express and Synchrony are reportedly bidding to take over JPMorgan Chase's Amazon co-branded credit card portfolio. JPMorgan Chase has partnered with Amazon on a Visa card program since 2002, but is now reportedly considering parting with the portfolio, which is valued at over $15 billion in loans. This move comes despite the card's success in offering perks like 5% cash back for Prime members and being one of the fastest-growing co-branded cards.

feed.title: Bancolombia migrará 100 % de su operación a Amazon Web Services
feed.summary: Bancolombia announced it will migrate 100% of its operations to Amazon Web Services (AWS) cloud services, investing $130 million to move 1,000 applications. This move is expected to reduce IT costs by 60% and has already improved data loading times and accounting processing efficiency. The bank aims to complete the migration by early 2024, continuing a relationship that began with the creation of Nequi in 2015.

feed.title: Securing Hybrid Cloud Containerized Workloads in AWS ECS Anywhere with SentinelOne Singularity
feed.summary: SentinelOne announced Day 1 support for AWS's Amazon Elastic Container Service (ECS) Anywhere, extending its Singularity Cloud Workload Security to hybrid cloud environments. This integration allows organizations to secure containerized workloads running on-premises and in AWS with SentinelOne's AI-driven protection, detection, and response capabilities. The solution aims to provide consistent operational models and elastic scalability for hybrid cloud deployments.

feed.title: NetApp brings ONTAP updates to simplify hybrid Cloud Data Management
feed.summary: NetApp has updated its ONTAP data management software to enhance features and simplify hybrid cloud data administration. This move aligns with NetApp's "cloud-first" strategy, aiming to unify managing public and on-premises cloud environments for better data access. The updates are expected to simplify storage procedures, improve management ease for IT administrators, and enhance overall performance and data protection capabilities.

feed.title: Staples Makes Plea to Buy Office Depot's Retail Operations
feed.summary: Staples is making its latest attempt to acquire Office Depot's consumer business for $1 billion. This renewed bid follows a failed $6-billion merger attempt in 2016 which was blocked by the FTC on antitrust grounds. Analysts suggest that even if successful, the merger might be too late, as both companies face intense competition from giants like Amazon and Walmart.

feed.title: TCL 5-Series Roku TV (S535) review: A killer QLED TV value
feed.summary: The TCL 5-Series Roku TV (S535) offers a strong 4K quantum-dot picture and built-in Roku smart features at a very competitive price, making it an excellent budget-friendly option. While it excels in color accuracy and gaming response time, its brightness and sound quality are moderate. This TV is highly recommended for those seeking a quality QLED experience without breaking the bank.

feed.title: Roblox sued for more than $200m by music publishers over licenses
feed.summary: A coalition of music publishers, led by the NMPA, has filed a lawsuit against the gaming platform Roblox, seeking over $200 million in damages for alleged widespread copyright infringement. NMPA President & CEO David Israelite accused Roblox of profiting from users uploading music without proper licenses and taking little action to prevent infringement. The NMPA also announced a significant escalation of its takedown campaign against Amazon-owned Twitch for similar licensing issues.

feed.title: Amazon, Walmart rivalry heats up with dueling prescription discounts
feed.summary: Amazon and Walmart are intensifying their rivalry by offering competing prescription discount programs to their respective Prime and Walmart+ members. Both companies launched similar initiatives to provide common medications at reduced prices or for free, aiming to attract and retain subscribers for their membership services. Amazon is offering six-month supplies of eligible medications starting at $1 per month for Prime members paying without insurance, while Walmart+ Rx for less provides discounts up to 85% off or free select medications for Walmart+ members.

feed.title: Amazon relaxes return-to-work plans, will let employees work remotely two days a week
feed.summary: Amazon has revised its return-to-work policy, allowing corporate employees to work remotely two days a week, a change from its previous office-centric stance. Employees will now be expected to be in the office three days a week, with the option for some to apply for exceptions or work fully remotely for up to four weeks annually. This move aligns Amazon with other tech companies like Apple and Google, which are also adopting hybrid work models.

feed.title: Zoom and Salesforce make quick $23 million profit after IPO of Israeli software vendor Monday.com
feed.summary: Zoom and Salesforce each invested $75 million into Monday.com's IPO, quickly realizing a combined profit of $23 million as the stock jumped 15% on its debut day. This investment strategy is part of a larger trend for Salesforce Ventures, which has yielded significant gains from other IPOs like Snowflake, while Zoom is also beginning to use investments as a new business venture. Both companies are subject to a 180-day lock-up period before they can sell their shares.

feed.title: K.K. Slider is coming to Build-A-Bear Workshop in the U.S.
feed.summary: K.K. Slider, the guitar-playing dog from Animal Crossing, is being released as a Build-A-Bear plushie in the U.S. This collaboration between Nintendo and Build-A-Bear Workshop previously offered Isabelle and Tom Nook. K.K. Slider will be available online only in the U.S., with a UK release planned for later.

feed.title: AMD: Enterprise Server Sales Will Catch Up With Overall EPYC Growth
feed.summary: AMD expects its EPYC server processor sales to enterprise customers to soon match the growth rate of its overall server CPU business, which doubled last year. Dan McNamara, head of AMD's EPYC business, highlighted that the company is gaining trust with CIOs and expanding its footprint with hyperscalers for internal workloads. This growth is driven by modernizing data centers, AMD's focus on solutions, and platform support from major OEMs, enabling AMD to better compete with Intel.

feed.title: Netgear Orbi WiFi 6 review: super-fast next-gen Wi-Fi, at a price
feed.summary: The Netgear Orbi WiFi 6 offers super-fast next-generation Wi-Fi with excellent coverage and speed, making it ideal for homes with many connected devices and Wi-Fi dead zones. While its high price and large unit size are drawbacks, the system provides quick setup and impressive performance, future-proofing home networks with Wi-Fi 6 support. It supports up to 100 devices simultaneously and delivers significantly faster download speeds in testing.

feed.title: Public wholesaler expands into Texas with largest distribution center to date
feed.summary: SiteOne Landscape Supply, a public wholesaler, has opened its largest distribution center to date in Hutchins, Texas. This facility marks the company's first in Texas and its fourth nationwide. The expansion highlights the growing industrial real estate activity in North Texas, following several significant warehouse and industrial park developments in the region.

feed.title: BlackBerry (BB) Stock Forecast: Long-Term Growth at a Reasonable Price
feed.summary: BlackBerry (BB) stock has seen significant volatility, doubling earlier in 2021 partly due to its status as a meme stock, though it has since pulled back. Despite short-term Reddit-fueled surges, the company has transformed into a security software specialist, focusing on cybersecurity and IoT for industries like autonomous cars. Analysts provide a lower price target, but the article suggests long-term growth potential due to Blackberry's strategic partnerships and QNX operating system's widespread integration in vehicles.

feed.title: Palo Alto Networks Enhances Cloud Security Posture Management (CSPM)
feed.summary: Palo Alto Networks has updated its Prisma Cloud solution for Cloud Security Posture Management (CSPM) to help organizations eliminate cybersecurity blind spots and guard against alert fatigue. These enhancements include True Internet Exposure, Visibility-as-Code, Network Data Exfiltration Detection, Anomalous Compute Provisioning Detection, and Customizable Object-Level Scanning for AWS S3. The CSPM market is experiencing significant growth, with revenues projected to reach $9 billion by 2026, driven by the high susceptibility of organizations to security breaches due to cloud misconfigurations.

feed.title: Coffee chain Starbucks applies for naming rights with US trademark office
feed.summary: Starbucks Corp. has applied with the U.S. trademark office for the right to use its name on a sports stadium or training facility, aiming to potentially join other major companies in sponsoring venues. The application, filed on June 2, seeks approval for "providing stadium and training facilities for sports and entertainment activities" and "promoting business, sports and entertainment events of others." The U.S. Patent and Trademark Office is expected to assign a lawyer to review the application by early September.

feed.title: Kraft Heinz CEO on the Ketchup Shortage
feed.summary: Miguel Patricio, CEO of Kraft Heinz, discusses the ongoing challenges of rising raw material prices, supply chain shocks, and product outages, including the significant ketchup-package shortage. He explains that the shortage is primarily for ketchup pouches due to increased takeout orders during the pandemic, and Kraft Heinz is addressing this by adding eight new production lines to increase capacity by 25% by July. Patricio also touches on broader inflationary pressures in raw materials, particularly edible oils, and how Kraft Heinz is adapting to consumer trends for less processed foods.

feed.title: Amazon.com Inc. snags second distribution building next to Nashville Superspeedway
feed.summary: Amazon.com Inc. has leased a second distribution building, a 257,000-square-foot facility, next to the Nashville Superspeedway in Lebanon. This new lease is adjacent to a 600,000-square-foot building Amazon is already leasing within the Speedway Industrial Park. The company is also constructing a 1.1 million-square-foot hub in Clarksville, significantly expanding its regional footprint.

feed.title: Online Exclusive: Shyft to launch its first EV chassis to meet demands of delivery companies
feed.summary: Shyft Group Inc. is developing its first all-electric chassis platform to meet growing demand from major parcel delivery customers like Amazon, FedEx, and UPS, with production set to begin in mid-2023. The company plans to invest between $50 million and $75 million in the initiative, aiming to produce 500-1,000 units in the first year. This move capitalizes on the increasing industry demand for electric delivery vehicles and builds on Shyft's successful transition to e-commerce truck manufacturing.

feed.title: Walmart's 'Deals for Days' event starts one day ahead of Prime Day
feed.summary: Walmart is launching its "Deals for Days" event on June 20, directly competing with Amazon's Prime Day which runs from June 21-22. This retail showdown includes other major retailers like Target, Kohl's, and Best Buy also hosting their own sales events around the same time. Walmart's spokesperson stated they often hold "Deals for Days" events, offering Black Friday-like savings in-store and online, differing from Amazon's online-only Prime membership requirement.

feed.title: California REIT buys Amazon fulfillment center in Fort Worth
feed.summary: Realty Income Corp., a California-based Real Estate Investment Trust, has purchased a 1.02 million-square-foot Amazon fulfillment center located in Fort Worth, Texas. This acquisition adds to the company's extensive portfolio, which already includes over 800 properties solely in Texas.

feed.title: Amazon and HanesBrands Team Up Against Counterfeiters in Flurry of Lawsuits
feed.summary: Amazon and HanesBrands have jointly filed multiple lawsuits against over two dozen China-based companies for allegedly selling counterfeit silicone earbud case covers bearing HanesBrands' "Champion" trademarks. The lawsuits aim to protect intellectual property, customers, and reputations, seeking injunctions, damages, and litigation costs. This action highlights Amazon's efforts to combat inauthentic products on its platform, while also noting recent legislative moves like the SHOP SAFE Act, which seeks to hold platforms accountable for counterfeit goods.

feed.title: Mall owner Washington Prime Group files for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection, citing COVID challenges
feed.summary: Mall owner Washington Prime Group Inc. filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection, citing the COVID-19 pandemic's impact on its operations. The company, which owns 102 shopping centers, has secured $100 million in debtor-in-possession financing to support day-to-day operations during the restructuring process. This move follows a challenging period for many retailers and mall operators, with projections indicating a significant number of malls may close in the coming years.

feed.title: Company bringing 390 jobs to Hazel Park in new 450K-square-foot facility
feed.summary: Mayville Engineering Company (MEC) is establishing its largest facility nationwide, a 450,000-square-foot plant, in Hazel Park, Michigan, at the Tri-County Commerce Center. This move is expected to create 390 well-paying jobs and involves an investment of up to $45 million in new operations, supported by a $2.5 million incentive package from the state. The facility, located on the site of the former Hazel Park Raceway, will significantly expand MEC's manufacturing capacity in Michigan and contribute to the city's tax revenue.

feed.title: Dimensional U.S. Core Equity 2 ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NYSEARCA:DFAC)
feed.summary: This article provides details on the Dimensional U.S. Core Equity 2 ETF (NYSEARCA:DFAC), an exchange-traded fund that invests in U.S. equities with a tilt towards small-cap companies. As of March 25, 2026, the ETF has a price of $39.21, assets under management of $40.41 billion, and a net expense ratio of 0.17%. The ETF's top holdings include major tech companies like NVIDIA, Apple, and Microsoft.

feed.title: Dimensional U.S. Core Equity 2 ETF
feed.summary: The Dimensional U.S. Core Equity 2 ETF (DFAC) is an actively managed ETF with $45.16 billion in assets, an expense ratio of 0.17%, and an inception date of June 14, 2021. It focuses on U.S. total market equities with a multi-cap strategy and currently has top holdings in major tech companies like NVIDIA, Apple, and Microsoft. The ETF has shown strong performance over 1-year and 3-year periods compared to its category average and targets an ESG score of 6.35/10.

feed.title: Airbnb and Booking targets of G7 push for higher taxes for multinationals and EU focus on restraining gatekeepers
feed.summary: The article discusses how Airbnb and Booking.com are becoming targets for higher taxation and stricter regulation due to evolving international policies. The G7 summit's proposal for a 15% global minimum corporate tax and a push for multinationals to pay taxes where they sell services directly challenges current practices of these platforms, which often pay lower taxes in countries like Ireland. Additionally, the EU's Digital Services Act aims to regulate large digital platforms like Airbnb and Booking.com as "gatekeepers," potentially leading to increased complexity for hosts and greater data sharing with municipalities.

feed.title: EPAM Builds Client Confidence Using AWS Training and Certification Initiative
feed.summary: EPAM Systems Inc. has invested in an AWS Training and Certification initiative for its global workforce to strengthen its cloud strategy and build client confidence. This program has led to hundreds of EPAM engineers achieving AWS certifications, enabling the company to offer more agile and proficient services as an AWS Advanced Consulting Partner. The initiative proves EPAM's expertise in AWS solutions and helps them establish good architectural habits for clients.

feed.title: Top Supply Chain Technology Themes in 2021
feed.summary: Gartner Inc. has identified the top 8 supply chain technology themes for 2021, focusing on broader themes rather than individual technologies to address specific business problems and foster operational resiliency. These themes, including Hyperautomation, Digital Supply Chain Twin, Immersive Experience, and Embedded AI, aim to drive innovation, improve decision-making, and enhance efficiency across supply chain networks. Christian Titze, a vice president analyst at Gartner, emphasizes their potential for transformative impact on business functions, technology, and service delivery.

feed.title: Growth Stocks: Roku Inc.
feed.summary: Roku Inc. (ROKU.US) is a US-based company known for its digital media players and streaming platform, which has seen significant growth in active users and revenue per user, especially during the COVID-19 pandemic. The company's business model transitioned from hardware sales to an advertising-driven platform, aligning with the "cable cutter" trend and achieving profitability. Technical analysis shows substantial price appreciation, with recent corrections finding support and indicating potential for further recovery towards previous highs.

feed.title: Cloud ramp up weighs on Oracle's profit view, shares fall
feed.summary: Oracle Corp's shares fell after the company forecast current-quarter profit below Wall Street estimates, heavily impacted by increased investments in its cloud computing business. The company plans to nearly double its capital spending on the cloud segment to take on rivals like Microsoft and Amazon, even as it sets up more data centers and wins high-profile customers like Zoom Video Communications. Despite beating fourth-quarter revenue and profit estimates, analysts still view Oracle as a niche player in the cloud market compared to leading infrastructure companies.

feed.title: Inside a factors-focused model portfolio
feed.summary: Tim Courtney, CIO of Exencial Wealth Advisors, discusses his firm's all-equity model portfolio, highlighting its focus on Dimensional Fund Advisors (DFA) funds for their factor-based weighting towards value, small companies, and profitability, despite recent underperformance. He also addresses portfolio diversification, managing overlap, and specific international fund selections like the DFA International Small Cap Value fund and American Funds EuroPacific Growth fund.

feed.title: Amazon and HanesBrands Team Up to File 13 Lawsuits Over the Sale of Counterfeit Champion Products
feed.summary: Amazon and HanesBrands have jointly filed over two dozen lawsuits against sellers on Amazon's marketplace for selling counterfeit Champion products. The lawsuits allege trademark infringement and false designation of origin, seeking monetary damages and injunctive relief to prevent further sales of unauthorized goods. This action is part of a broader effort by Amazon to combat counterfeits and build consumer trust, particularly as issues with seller identity and legislative challenges surrounding the Inform Consumers Act have emerged.

feed.title: Amazon Announces New Robotics Fulfillment Center in Baton Rouge
feed.summary: Amazon plans to open its second robotics fulfillment center in Louisiana, located in Baton Rouge. This new 820,000 square-foot facility will create over 1,000 full-time jobs, offering competitive wages starting at $15 per hour and comprehensive benefits. The investment underscores Amazon's continued expansion in Louisiana, contributing to the local economy and revitalizing the former Cortana Mall site.

feed.title: Cybercriminals go after Amazon Prime Day Shoppers
feed.summary: Cybercriminals are targeting Amazon Prime Day shoppers by registering thousands of malicious and suspicious domains related to Amazon. Check Point Research found that almost half of new Amazon-related domains are malicious, and a significant portion of Amazon Prime related domains are also malicious or suspicious. The article provides examples of phishing attempts and offers practical security tips to help shoppers stay safe from these scams.

feed.title: Bancolombia y Amazon Web Services firman uno de los negocios del año en materia de tecnología. ¿En qué consiste?
feed.summary: Bancolombia, Colombia's largest bank, is migrating 100% of its applications to Amazon Web Services (AWS) cloud, aiming to benefit over 20 million clients across multiple countries. This significant technological step, involving a US$130 million investment over three years, seeks to enhance security, agility, and availability of digital platforms while reducing operational costs by approximately 60%. The bank is also investing in training its employees for cloud computing skills to support this transition.

feed.title: Cloud-native backup benefits and use cases explained
feed.summary: Cloud-native backup offers organizations fast, reliable, and scalable data protection for workloads on containers, VMs, and hyperscaler services. It leverages native cloud capabilities for cost-effective disaster recovery, compliance, and ransomware protection. Key benefits include automation, self-management, API-driven policy adjustments, and metadata enrichment for efficient storage and data discovery.

feed.title: Pinnacle Financial Partners relocating headquarters to Nashville Yards location
feed.summary: Pinnacle Financial Partners is relocating its corporate headquarters to the Nashville Yards location in downtown Nashville. The new headquarters will anchor a multi-tenant commercial office building and will also include a 10,000 square foot retail presence and an all-digital office concept within Amazon Nashville. The building is designed to be 34 stories tall with over 650,000 square feet of office space.

feed.title: Kroger sees strong private label, digital sales even as pandemic surge fades
feed.summary: Kroger reported a decline in identical-store and overall sales in Q1 2021 compared to the pandemic-fueled surge of Q1 2020, but showed significant growth when compared to Q1 2019. Despite the overall decline, the company saw strong digital sales growth and momentum in its private label business. Kroger is optimistic about continued consumer shifts favoring at-home cooking and premium products, and plans to double its digital business in the coming years.

feed.title: 10 Ways To Get Noticed By Amazon: Why This Recruiter Believes That Connection Is Key
feed.summary: Theo Burton, a talent manager and Diversity Ambassador at Amazon, shares 10 key tips for job seekers, especially Black candidates, to get noticed and hired at competitive companies like Amazon. He emphasizes the importance of networking, demonstrating measurable results, connecting with decision-makers, and being prepared for negotiations. Burton also highlights that the current post-Covid job market, and particularly the push for diversity, presents a significant opportunity for candidates of color.

feed.title: Amazon Prime Day starts soon. These are the top deals so far
feed.summary: Amazon Prime Day is set to begin on June 21, featuring discounts on over 2 million items for Prime members. EMarketer forecasts total digital sales in the U.S. will reach $12.18 billion during the event, with Amazon-exclusive sales projected at $7.31 billion. Beyond Amazon's own products, other retailers like Walmart, Target, and Best Buy will offer competing deals, and consumers are advised to plan purchases to avoid overspending.

feed.title: How Etsy went from quirky crafts website to glossy e-commerce powerhouse
feed.summary: Etsy has transformed from a niche online craft store into a sprawling e-commerce company valued at $20 billion, evidenced by its acquisition of Depop. This evolution has involved significant growing pains, including leadership changes, shifts in policy regarding handmade goods, and balancing the interests of sellers with those of shareholders. Despite some resistance from its original "homespun ethos" fanbase, Etsy's strategic moves, particularly under CEO Josh Silverman, have focused on driving growth and profitability, aligning it more closely with major e-commerce players like Amazon.

feed.title: CVS and Walgreens were reeling. Now they’re riding a COVID-19 wave.
feed.summary: Pharmacy giants CVS and Walgreens, initially struggling during the pandemic, have transformed COVID-19 into a significant revenue driver. They are now profiting from increased vaccine administration fees, in-store sales from vaccine recipients, and the sale of various COVID-19 testing products. This shift has led to improved financial forecasts and a rebound in their stock prices, validating their healthcare hub strategies.

feed.title: Americans are dining out again. Target wants to lure them to the grocery aisle with deals
feed.summary: Target is adding food and beverages to its Deal Days sales event, aiming to attract consumers back to its grocery aisles even as dining out resumes. This strategic move, which includes discounts and a gift card promotion, marks the first time Target is using the event to promote groceries, a category that significantly boosted its sales during the pandemic. The initiative is a direct response to Amazon Prime Day and reflects a broader effort by grocers like Walmart and Amazon to retain elevated grocery spending amidst shifting consumer habits.

feed.title: Bill Gates Says Every Leader Should Read This Book This Summer
feed.summary: Bill Gates recommends "Lights Out: Pride, Delusion, and the Fall of General Electric" as essential summer reading for leaders. The book, written by Wall Street Journal reporters, details GE's decline, offering cautionary lessons on leadership, particularly regarding the dangers of fixating on short-term profits. Gates emphasizes that focusing solely on quarterly earnings can lead to systemic weaknesses, contrasting GE's approach with companies like Amazon and Tesla that prioritized long-term growth over immediate analyst expectations.

feed.title: Realty Income Delivers High Yields and Dependable Dividend Growth
feed.summary: This article analyzes Realty Income (O), a real estate investment trust, highlighting its high yields and consistent dividend growth. The author examines its business model of triple-net leases, tenant concentration, occupancy rates, and FFO trends over the past decade. The piece also discusses potential risks from changing retail landscapes and rising interest rates, while noting the company's strong management, conservative balance sheet, and upcoming acquisition of Vereit (VER).

feed.title: Amazon’s Prime Day prompts Walmart, Target, Best Buy deals galore
feed.summary: Amazon's Prime Day is prompting major retailers like Walmart, Target, Kohl's, and Best Buy to launch their own competing sales events. These retailers are strategically timing their discount deals, with many overlapping Prime Day, to attract customers and capture a share of the heightened shopping activity. The article highlights the "retail wars" as these companies aim to capitalize on the sales momentum generated by Amazon's annual event.

feed.title: Will Intuitive Surgical Be a Trillion-Dollar Stock by 2031?
feed.summary: This article analyzes whether Intuitive Surgical (ISRG) can achieve a trillion-dollar market capitalization by 2031. It compares Intuitive's current market cap and growth trajectory to trillion-dollar tech companies, noting its dominance in robotic surgery market but also potential challenges from rivals and revenue growth limitations. Although reaching $1 trillion is deemed unlikely, the author remains optimistic about Intuitive Surgical as a strong long-term investment for healthcare portfolios.

feed.title: Amazon in Talks to Buy Stake in AI Truck-Driving Startup Plus
authors: Lulu Yilun Chen and Gillian Tan
feed.summary: Amazon.com Inc. has ordered 1,000 autonomous driving systems from Plus, an AI truck-driving startup, and secured an option to purchase up to a 20% stake in the company. This deal allows Amazon to buy preferred shares at approximately 47 cents per share, potentially valuing Plus at $3.3 billion after its merger with Hennessy Capital Investment Corp. V. Plus, backed by Sequoia Capital China, is developing autonomous technology for longhaul trucking and is expanding its partnerships and operations in the U.S. and China.

feed.title: Amazon Snaps Up Option to Buy Stake in AI Truck-Driving Startup
authors: Lulu Yilun Chen and Gillian Tan
feed.summary: Amazon has ordered 1,000 autonomous driving systems from the self-driving truck technology startup Plus and secured an option to acquire up to a 20% stake in the company. This move allows the e-commerce giant to purchase preferred shares through a warrant at a set price, contingent on Plus's planned merger with Hennessy Capital Investment Corp. V. The deal was confirmed by Plus in a regulatory filing, corroborating an earlier Bloomberg report.

feed.title: Amazon Prime Day deals expected to disappoint as costs surge
feed.summary: Amazon Prime Day deals are expected to be less attractive this year due to surging costs, including rising shipping expenses, higher advertising rates, and scarce inventory. Merchants are holding back on deep discounts and inventory, anticipating that supply-chain disruptions will continue through the holiday season. Despite this, online spending in the U.S. during the event is still projected to set a record at $12.2 billion, with Amazon capturing 60%.

feed.title: Why IBM, GlobalFoundries are suing each other over East Fishkill deal
feed.summary: IBM and GlobalFoundries are suing each other over a 2014 deal where IBM paid GlobalFoundries $1.5 billion to take over its microchip plants and supply chips. IBM claims GlobalFoundries failed to provide suitable chips and is seeking the money back plus damages, while GlobalFoundries counters that IBM's demands required billions more in investment and accuses IBM of seeking a "quick payday" following news of its potential IPO. The dispute centers on GlobalFoundries' decision to halt development of certain high-performance chips.

feed.title: Vera Bradley up to 66% off: Purses, tote bags, wallets, backpacks, duffel, cosmetic and lunch bags
feed.summary: Vera Bradley products, including purses, tote bags, wallets, and backpacks, are on sale for up to 66% off as part of Amazon Prime Day deals. The article also highlights various other Prime Day discounts across electronics, home goods, clothing, toys, and more, encouraging readers to sign up for a free Prime membership to access these savings.

feed.title: Amazon is in talks to buy stake in AI truck-driving startup Plus
authors: Lulu Yilun Chen and Gillian Tan
feed.summary: Amazon.com Inc. has placed an order for 1,000 autonomous driving systems from Plus, an AI truck-driving technology startup. Amazon is also in talks to acquire a stake of up to 20% in Plus, with a warrant enabling them to buy preferred shares at $0.46647 per share, as confirmed by a company filing. This potential deal would give Amazon a significant interest in Plus before its planned merger with Hennessy Capital Investment Corp. V.

feed.title: Big U.S. retailers line up deals to take on Amazon Prime Day frenzy
feed.summary: Major U.S. retailers such as Target, Walmart, Bed Bath & Beyond, Macy's, and Kohl's are launching significant sales events to compete with Amazon's annual Prime Day. These retailers are offering big discounts and promotions to capitalize on the increased consumer spending during the two-day event, which is taking place earlier this year. Adobe anticipates that Prime Day sales for U.S. retailers will exceed $11 billion, surpassing last year's Black Friday and Cyber Monday figures.

feed.title: Check Point CloudGuard Integrates with AWS CloudFormation Public Registry at launch
feed.summary: Check Point CloudGuard has integrated with the new AWS CloudFormation Public Registry at its launch to enhance automation for cloud security users. This integration allows Check Point to publish extensions into a searchable catalog, simplifying the deployment of CloudGuard Network Security. The article explains AWS CloudFormation and its Public Registry, highlighting how this new functionality streamlines cloud resource provisioning and management for customers.

feed.title: Post-Pandemic Media Consumption: Online Streaming Accelerates A New Content Experience
feed.summary: The COVID-19 pandemic significantly accelerated the shift to online media consumption, with streaming services becoming a primary source of entertainment. This trend has reshaped consumer expectations, leading to continued heavy TV/video consumption, a decline in offline media use, and a move away from movie theaters towards at-home viewing. Consumers are also seeking to replicate public viewing experiences within private social circles.

feed.title: IAS IPO News - Vista Equity's ad verification platform Integral Ad Science sets terms for $240 million IPO
feed.summary: Integral Ad Science Holding, an ad verification platform backed by Vista Equity, has announced terms for its initial public offering, aiming to raise $240 million. The company, which uses AI and machine learning to verify digital ad performance and brand safety, plans to offer 15 million shares at a price range of $15 to $17. Integral Ad Science booked $254 million in sales for the 12 months ended March 31, 2021, and will list on the Nasdaq under the symbol IAS.

feed.title: Hewlett Packard Enterprise Acquires AI Company Determined AI to Focus on Accelerating Machine Learning
feed.summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) has acquired AI startup Determined AI to accelerate machine learning models. Determined AI, founded by UC Berkeley researchers, specializes in open-source machine learning platforms that significantly reduce computation time for AI model training. This acquisition aims to combine HPE's high-performance computing with Determined AI's technology to simplify AI development and shorten time to market.

feed.title: FTC to review Amazon’s planned $8.45 billion purchase of MGM: report
feed.summary: The Federal Trade Commission will review Amazon.com Inc.’s proposed $8.45 billion acquisition of MGM studios. This review will be conducted under the FTC's new chair, Lina Khan, a known critic of Amazon’s expansion. The deal aims to significantly boost content on Amazon Prime Video, incorporating franchises like James Bond and TV series such as “The Handmaid’s Tale.”

feed.title: Amazon Prime Day is over but deals still exist. Here's how I've gotten $4,700 in rebates and free stuff
feed.summary: This article describes how to find significant discounts on Amazon and other online stores even after major shopping events like Prime Day. The author highlights RebateKey.com as a method to secure rebates and free items without the usual proof-of-purchase hurdles, claiming to have received $4,700 in rebates and free stuff.

feed.title: With $200M from private equity, Saks Off 5th online will go it alone
feed.summary: Hudson's Bay Company (HBC) is spinning off the digital business of Saks Off 5th into a standalone entity, backed by $200 million from Insight Partners and Rhône. The brick-and-mortar stores, with 105 locations, will remain wholly owned by HBC under the name "O5," operating in a franchise-like relationship with the new digital company. This move mirrors HBC's earlier separation of Saks Fifth Avenue's online and physical operations, signifying a strategy to unbundle its retail assets.

feed.title: Jessica Alba’s Favorite Baby Wipes and More Are On Sale at Amazon Prime Day
feed.summary: Jessica Alba, co-founder of The Honest Company, shared her top Amazon Prime Day picks, including her brand's baby wipes and beauty products, as well as various items from small businesses. The article highlights several of these favorite products, ranging from noise-canceling headphones and reusable containers to children's toys and handmade jewelry, all available at discounted prices during the event.

feed.title: PepsiCo to launch line of premium, non-alcoholic cocktail mixers
feed.summary: PepsiCo is introducing Unmuddled, a new line of premium, non-alcoholic cocktail mixers, in early September. These mixers are made without artificial sweeteners and come in three varieties: Lemon Mint, Fiery Pineapple, and Spiced Mandarin. This launch follows PepsiCo's earlier introduction of Neon Zebra non-alcoholic cocktail mixers in March.

feed.title: Amazon and Apple: A tale of two closed-loop visions
feed.summary: This article contrasts the circular economy visions of Amazon and Apple, detailing their different approaches to sustainability despite their massive scale and technological leadership. It highlights Amazon's focus on customer education and packaging reduction, and Apple's emphasis on supply chain engagement, material recycling, and product design for circularity. Both companies integrate social justice into their sustainability efforts, supporting initiatives for underserved communities.

feed.title: S&P ETF Fortunes Reverse In 2021
feed.summary: In 2021, S&P 500 ETFs with alternative weighting methodologies, such as reverse market cap (YPS) and equal weight (RSP), began outperforming the traditional market-cap-weighted SPDR S&P 500 Trust (SPY). This shift is attributed to the underperformance of large-cap tech stocks and the better performance of mid-cap and smaller companies benefiting from the U.S. economic reopening. While alternative strategies show short-term gains, SPY maintains long-term outperformance and significant cost advantages due to its size and liquidity.

feed.title: Wattpad And Webtoon Merge Studios With $100 Million In Financing
feed.summary: Wattpad and Webtoon have merged their studio divisions to form Wattpad Webtoon Studios, backed by $100 million in financing from their parent company, Naver. The new studio aims to leverage combined IP libraries and creators to produce global fan and data-driven TV shows, films, and books with over 100 projects already in development or production. This strategic move strengthens Naver's global position in IP, digital comics, and web novels.

feed.title: Focus: Bezos' 2021 Space Odyssey a risk too far for insurers
authors: Noor Zainab Hussain and Carolyn Cohn
feed.summary: Insurers are hesitant to cover the risks associated with Jeff Bezos's impending space flight and commercial space tourism in general, despite a nearly $500 million market for insuring satellites and unmanned flights. There are currently no legal requirements for operators like Blue Origin to insure passengers for injury or death, and the industry is grappling with whether space travel risks fall under space or aviation insurance lines. However, companies like Global Aerospace have begun receiving inquiries for sub-orbital missions, suggesting this nascent market may evolve substantially in the coming years.

feed.title: Amazon has spent billions to get within a 1-hour delivery distance of many U.S. customers, but Walmart and Target are still winning that race
feed.summary: Despite Amazon investing $35 billion in 2020 to build out its delivery infrastructure, Walmart and Target, with their extensive network of brick-and-mortar stores, remain closer to more U.S. customers for swift delivery. This suggests that the physical presence of traditional retailers still provides a competitive edge in rapid fulfillment, even against Amazon's substantial e-commerce investments.

feed.title: Microsoft challenges Apple's business model with new Windows 11 operating system
feed.summary: Microsoft unveiled Windows 11, its first major operating system update since 2015, which directly challenges Apple's lucrative App Store model. The new Windows Store will allow developers to use their own in-app payment systems without commissions and enable Android apps to run on Windows PCs. This move contrasts sharply with Apple's "walled garden" approach and also competes with Google Play Store's commission structure, signaling a significant shift in Microsoft's strategy to attract developers and consumers.

feed.title: Ford's chief technology officer to leave company for job at Amazon
feed.summary: Ken Washington, Ford's chief technology officer, is departing the company on July 16 to take a role as vice president of software engineering at Amazon. His departure highlights an ongoing "war for talent" between the auto and tech industries. Jim Buczkowski will serve as interim head of Ford's research and advanced engineering team.

feed.title: Ford Motor Co. loses chief technology officer Ken Washington to Amazon
feed.summary: Ford's Chief Technology Officer Ken Washington is leaving the automaker to join Amazon as vice president of software engineering. His departure leaves a void in crucial areas like next-generation vehicle electrical architectures and advanced materials, with Jim Buczkowski taking interim responsibility. Washington, who has been CTO since June 2017, played a significant role in Ford's technology strategy, including autonomous vehicle efforts.

feed.title: PBS FY22 budget increases station dues, spending on content and marketing
feed.summary: PBS's board approved a fiscal year 2022 budget increasing station dues and expenses for content and marketing, totaling $339.1 million. The budget allocates $243.4 million for content and marketing and projects $203.7 million in station dues revenue, with PBS President Paula Kerger emphasizing adapting to the competitive media landscape and focusing on diversity initiatives. This includes a commitment to fund programs by nonfiction filmmakers of color and recruiting a head of diversity, equity, and inclusion.

feed.title: Simpson Manufacturing announces the appointment of Kenneth Knight to its board of directors
feed.summary: Simpson Manufacturing Co., Inc. has appointed Kenneth Knight as a non-employee director to its board, where he will also serve on the audit and finance committee. Knight brings extensive experience from roles at Invitae Corporation, Amazon.com, Inc., General Motors Company, and Caterpillar Inc., which is expected to be instrumental for Simpson's growth initiatives. James Andrasick, chair of Simpson’s board, highlighted Knight’s background in industrial manufacturing, M&A, and supply chain management as key assets.

feed.title: Shinsegae now Korea’s No.2 e-commerce player with eBay Korea buyout
feed.summary: Shinsegae Group's E-Mart Inc. has acquired an 80% stake in eBay Korea for 3.44 trillion won ($3.04 billion), making Shinsegae the second-largest e-commerce player in South Korea, surpassing Coupang. This acquisition boosts Shinsegae's e-commerce market share to 15% and total transactions to 24 trillion won ($21.2 billion), placing it behind Naver. The deal is significant for the retail sector, with Shinsegae actively pursuing financing options including borrowing from banks and potentially issuing corporate bonds to fund the acquisition.

feed.title: Payoneer Is Set to Merge With FTOC, Future Looks Bright
feed.summary: Payoneer is set to go public through a reverse merger with the SPAC FTAC Olympus Acquisition Corp. (FTOC), with common stock expected to trade under "PAYO" on Nasdaq starting June 28. The company, which provides payment services for major firms like Amazon and Google, shows strong financials with expected 2021 revenues of $432 million and a reasonable valuation, making it a potentially attractive investment. Payoneer's business was significantly boosted by the COVID-19 pandemic and the shift to online transactions, with impressive growth reported in Q1 2021, and the company has recently undergone a rebranding effort.

feed.title: Massive 1.1 million-square-foot warehouse planned for Linglestown Road
feed.summary: Prologis, a major warehouse company, plans to build a 1.1 million-square-foot warehouse in West Hanover Township on Linglestown Road. The plans were tabled by the West Hanover Township Planning Commission due to unresolved concerns about stormwater, buffer requirements, and traffic from the township engineer and residents. The location was chosen due to its proximity to major transportation routes, serving the growing e-commerce industry.

feed.title: Disney uses HPE for private cloud
feed.summary: The Walt Disney Company continues to utilize Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) technology for its internal private cloud infrastructure, even as it has adopted Amazon Web Services (AWS) for its Disney+ streaming service. Disney maintains this long-running partnership due to the need for responsive internal servers, particularly for its diverse operations ranging from theme parks to stage shows. The company leverages HPE's Synergy composable infrastructure and VMware Cloud Foundation to provide cloud-like experiences with local resources for its "cast members" and support data-intensive operations at its theme parks.

feed.title: Tech Moves: T-Mobile adds former U.S. intelligence director to board; Amazon execs depart; and more
feed.summary: T-Mobile has appointed former National Geospatial-Intelligence Agency Director Letitia Long to its board, where she will serve on the nominating and corporate governance committee and as national security director. This "Tech Moves" article also details several Amazon executive departures and new hires, including Rudy Valdez leaving AWS, Dorothy Li joining Convoy as CTO, and former Amazon veterans Tim Collins and Sharad Sundaresan taking roles at Gopuff. Other notable personnel changes include Jean-Olivier Racine joining Outset Medical, Chris Min becoming Skytap's new CFO, Mike Cartwright's appointment at Snap! Raise, Marie Huwe moving to Dolby.io, Ruchika Tulshyan joining Reverb's advisory board, and Ben Motazed's new role at Aerovel.

feed.title: Apple Explores Bigger iPads and Reshuffles Its Car Team
feed.summary: Apple is reportedly exploring the development of larger iPad models and making significant changes to its secretive Apple Car project. Additionally, the company Peloton is positioning itself to compete with the Apple Watch in the wearable technology market, while Amazon is delving into augmented reality.

feed.title: How Western Union is fighting back against fintech startups
feed.summary: Western Union, a long-standing player in cross-border payments, is actively combating competition from fintech startups by heavily investing in its digital operations. The company saw significant growth in digital money transfer revenues in 2020 due to the pandemic-driven acceleration towards digital services, and expects revenues to exceed $1 billion in 2021. Western Union is also expanding its offerings beyond payments, including launching a bank account, debit card, and multicurrency accounts in Europe, and modernizing its technology using cloud platforms and AI.

feed.title: Bank Bucks U.S. Peers, Saying No One Should Return 5 Days a Week
feed.summary: Synchrony Financial (SYF), a major credit card issuer, is embracing a flexible remote work model, opting against a full five-day in-office return for its employees, unlike many of its U.S. banking peers. The company has begun testing its reopening plan with a limited number of employees but is largely committed to remote work and reducing office space. This decision contrasts with banks like Goldman Sachs and JPMorgan Chase, which are pushing for a broader return to the office.

feed.title: Online payment company Payoneer goes public after SPAC merger
feed.summary: Payments provider Payoneer went public on the Nasdaq stock exchange after closing a merger with a special-purpose acquisition company (SPAC) backed by fintech entrepreneur Betsy Cohen. The deal valued the company at about $3.3 billion, and Payoneer expects significant revenue growth in 2021 due to accelerated digital commerce. The company plans to use its new public currency for bolder investments and acquisitions to expand its services.

feed.title: How Salesforce, Amazon, Google measure climate impact
feed.summary: This article discusses how companies like Salesforce, Amazon, and Google are measuring their climate impact and the challenges involved. It highlights the increasing pressure on companies to demonstrate sustainability efforts through trusted data and metrics, moving beyond just reporting to actively using data to drive decarbonization. The piece also emphasizes the importance of internal communication, incentives, and policymaker involvement in achieving climate goals.
